133741.explanation: Residents should be seriously involved in decisions that affect the place where they live. We believe in participation through neighbourhood dialogues, citizen panels, and other forms of consultation. Referendums could be used as an additional instrument, subject to careful preparation and clear safeguards.

133742.explanation: Property tax (OZB) needs to be distributed more fairly. Residents on low incomes should be partly or fully exempted, to keep living costs affordable. At the same time, the municipality needs to retain sufficient funds to invest in quality amenities and liveable neighbourhoods that benefit all residents.

133743.explanation: Residents know best what their neighbourhoods need, and we want to give neighbourhood and village councils a say in the plans that affect their area. Not just a feedback opportunity, but real joint decision-making. This way we can make political choices that do justice to the diversity of Zaanstad, and have community support.

133746.explanation: Ground lease must not be a source of uncertainty or financial stress. Giving residents an affordable option to buy out their ground lease, with due consideration for affordability and fair treatment, will create housing security and cost predictability.

133747.explanation: We want to make room for businesses with sustainable, clean, and future-minded operations. Existing industrial estates must first be made more efficient and sustainable before we build new ones. Space is also scarce, and we give priority to housing and liveability.

133748.explanation: A healthy living environment is a fundamental right. Companies that pollute must be called to account, and enforcement action taken where necessary. Stricter enforcement protects residents, the environment, and employees, and ensures fair competition for businesses who do have sustainable and people-centred operations.

133749.explanation: The environment in and around Zaanstad is under threat, and must be protected. Industrial growth must not be to the detriment of greenery and nature, but should occur within existing estates through more intelligent and sustainable use of space.

133750.explanation: Everyone in urgent need of housing deserves a fair chance. We consider it important that homes are fairly distributed on a needs basis. Recognised refugees also have a right to proper housing. We would like to provide adequate affordable homes for all groups, however.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad is meeting its responsibilities when it comes to refugee shelter, and does so out of solidarity and compassion. Our municipality is taking on large numbers of refugees, and we believe it is time for others to step up and do the same.

133752.explanation: Waste separation should be easy and affordable for all. Pay-per-use primarily impacts people on low incomes, and increases the risk of litter and side-dumping.

133753.explanation: Zaanstad needs a healthy and future-proof economy. That means that new businesses will need to contribute to sustainability, a clean living environment, and proper working conditions. Companies that pollute or take no responsibility for people or planet have no place here.

133754.explanation: The energy transition requires additional space to generate sustainable energy. Solar panels on agricultural land can contribute, with suitable care and respect for the landscape.

133756.explanation: The climate crisis demands urgency. The sooner we invest in sustainability, the greater the benefit to the climate, living environment, and residents’ finances. We are committed to a social and just approach, with extra consideration for households that cannot bear the cost themselves.

133757.explanation: To combat climate change and keep energy bills affordable, all homes must ultimately become gas-free. Our plan is to ensure that this transition is socially fair and manageable, with additional support for low-income households and homes where sustainability options are limited.

133758.explanation: Wind energy is indispensable in the generation of sustainable energy. Locations for new wind turbines must be chosen carefully, with attention to landscape, nature, and the surrounding residents. Local property and public consultation are important, to ensure that residents can share in the benefits.

133759.explanation: The polders around Assendelft and Westzaan are precious landscapes, and the new high-voltage power lines must be avoided there at all costs. Alternatives, such as bundling infrastructure or underground solutions, are preferable in order to preserve nature and quality of life.

133760.explanation: Night flights cause serious noise pollution and health problems for the local residents. Zaanstad and other municipalities must take serious action to reduce or stop the night flights. The health and sleep of residents must take priority over economic interests.

133761.explanation: Sports associations play an indispensable role in health, community, and social cohesion. Clubs in financial trouble can impact neighbourhoods and young people. The municipality should therefore provide support where necessary, so that sport remains accessible to all.

133763.explanation: An event site could promote culture, community and a lively region, provided a suitable location is found. A permanent site eliminates the need for temporary locations, and reduces disturbance for local neighbourhoods. Good accessibility and early involvement of surrounding residents will be essential in this regard: it must not infringe on nature or residents’ peace and quiet.

133764.explanation: We believe residents must be able to play sports and exercise in their own neighbourhoods. Outdoor sports promote health, community, and a pleasant neighbourhood.

133765.explanation: The municipality should contribute to safe, accessible and sustainable sports facilities, since they make us all healthier. They also promote social contact, making neighbourhoods safer and more pleasant.

133766.explanation: We believe it is important to preserve heritage and other authentic buildings, as they contribute to the identity and historic character of our region. It is important to carefully assess which buildings deserve protection, balancing historical value, neighbourhood interests, and future viability.

133767.explanation: Neighbourhood safety begins with street visibility and presence. More BOAs ensure increased supervision, rapid response, and greater approachability. They can spot early signs, de-escalate, and make streets safer.

133768.explanation: Security cameras can contribute to safety, especially where there is frequent disturbance or crime. It should not be an end in itself, however. Cameras must be deployed in a considered and targeted fashion, with clear privacy safeguards and always in combination with human surveillance.

133770.explanation: The growth in knife-carrying by young people is worrying, and demands preventive action. Education makes young people aware of the risks and consequences. This, in conjunction with youth support services, schools, and parents, will help to prevent violence.

133771.explanation: Disruptive behaviour among youth is often a sign of underlying issues, such as a lack of opportunities or guidance. Investing in youth services, sport, culture and support can lead to a lasting reduction in disruptive behaviour, and give young people more development opportunities.

133772.explanation: The need for housing in Zaanstad is high, and scaling back building plans will only increase the problem. New homes must be carefully considered, however, with attention to nature, amenities, and accessibility.

133773.explanation: For people on medium incomes, it is becoming harder and harder to find a suitable home. Focusing on building more mid-range rental and owner-occupied homes will improve mobility in the housing market and prevent this group from falling between the cracks.

133774.explanation: Greenery helps combat heat stress and flooding, and contributes to biodiversity. By offering financial incentives for garden greening, the municipality encourages neighbourhood-level climate adaptation and creates a healthier, more attractive living environment.

133775.explanation: Demolition and new development can be necessary for buildings not suitable for energy refitting or for engineering reasons. Reuse and renovations should always be investigated first, however. New builds must be sustainable, future-proof, and well-integrated into the surroundings.

133776.explanation: Neighbourhoods can only function effectively with proper basic facilities nearby. They make for a pleasant neighbourhood and a nice place to live. New neighbourhoods must therefore be developed holistically, with sufficient amenities from the start.

133777.explanation: Unchecked subdivision can lead to disturbance, lack of safety, and displacement of regular households. The municipality must establish and enforce clear regulations, to preserve housing quality and prevent illegal activities.

133778.explanation: It is important that people who have lived in Zaanstad for a long time or who have a strong connection to the area retain access to affordable housing in their own municipality. At the same time, there must also be options for people in urgent need of housing.

133779.explanation: The housing shortage primarily affects people on low incomes. Committing to at least 40% rental social housing will prevent Zaanstad from becoming unaffordable for large groups of residents, contributing to diverse neighbourhoods and social cohesion.

133780.explanation: Mandatory owner-occupation prevents market speculation, and ensures that affordable homes remain accessible to starters and families instead of investors, helping to keep the market fair.

133781.explanation: Intelligently combining housing and employment can help combat the housing shortage without putting additional pressure on the landscape. Living and working environments must be effectively separated where necessary, however, and quality of the living environment safeguarded.

133782.explanation: Villages adjoin natural areas, which must be protected. Adequate housing sometimes means expansion is unavoidable, but it should never be the first option.

133783.explanation: Poverty impacts daily life and participation in society. Combating poverty improves financial security, and prevents stress and debt. By supporting households with incomes up to 130% of the social minimum, we prevent people from falling through the cracks. Prevention is cheaper.

133784.explanation: People on welfare benefits often want to take part in society, but do not always have a direct opportunity to do so. Mandatory mutual participation can often be detrimental, increasing stress and distrust.

133785.explanation: The accessibility of Wormerveer and Krommenie is at risk. An additional bridge across the Zaan could contribute to improved traffic flow and road safety. At the same time, a project like this must be adequately researched, with a view to liveability, the environment, and noise pollution.

133786.explanation: Proper accessibility does not stop at midnight. For people working in healthcare, hospitality or industry, reliable public transport is essential. Opening the bus bridge 24 hours will contribute to accessibility, safety, and the effective functioning of the region.

133787.explanation: New roads often mean more traffic and emissions, while space is limited. A better focus, therefore, is improved public transport, effective bicycle routes, and intelligent mobility solutions. These will ensure the city remains an accessible, healthy, and pleasant place to live.

133788.explanation: The train line creates a physical and mental barrier in the city. Putting it underground will reduce noise pollution, increase safety, and provide space for housing and nature. Though costly, it can offer major long-term benefits for liveability and cohesion in Zaanstad.

133790.explanation: A night train will improve accessibility for people who work or go out at night. It will increase safety, reduce car traffic, and contribute to the vision for sustainable mobility.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 connection would be detrimental to nature, the living environment, and the health of surrounding residents. It is a costly project, with little benefit to accessibility. We would prefer to focus on improved public transport, innovative traffic measures, and investing in a healthier living environment.

133792.explanation: The Vlinderbrug bridge was made for slow traffic, and offers a safe connection for cyclists and pedestrians. Opening it to cars would compromise road safety and liveability in the surrounding neighbourhoods. We consciously favour safe, low-traffic connections.

133793.explanation: Residents must be able to rely on parking close to their home. For many people – such as families, senior citizens and people dependent on their car for work or care – adequate parking is essential.

133794.explanation: Paid parking is an important instrument in the fair distribution of public space and to minimise problems. Unnecessarily low rates lead to additional traffic and parking congestion. The system must still be socially fair, however, with consideration for residents, people with disabilities, and those on low incomes.

133795.explanation: Air pollution is harmful to health, especially that of children and the elderly. A low-emissions zone contributes to cleaner air and a healthier living environment. Keeping out high-polluting vehicles while also supporting the transition to cleaner transport will make Zaanstad ready for the future.

133796.explanation: Lower speeds reduce road accidents, lower noise pollution, and create a more pleasant living environment. 30km/h is now the norm in many cities, and is in line with efforts to create a safe and pleasant living environment.

133797.explanation: Young people need safe and accessible places to meet. They prevent disruptive behaviour, increase social cohesion, and provide opportunities for development. Municipalities should actively foster these spaces, along with the young people themselves.

133798.explanation: Care close to home is essential, especially for an ageing population. Community care centres allow support, social contact, and care to be combined, and increase social cohesion in villages and neighbourhoods.

133799.explanation: Informal carers provide indispensable care, significantly reducing strain on professional care organisations. A higher token of appreciation is a form of recognition for their efforts, and helps them to maintain their work.

133800.explanation: Effective preparation for pregnancy and parenthood contributes to a healthy start for children. By offering free classes, we lower the barriers and invest in preventive healthcare.

133801.explanation: With so much water in the Netherlands, water safety is essential. All children should learn to swim, whatever their parents’ income. Free swimming lessons foster equal opportunity, and prevent drowning.

133741.explanation: We need clear regulations to prevent abuse of the instrument. Do not forget that a referendum costs €400,000 – you don't want one every week.

133742.explanation: All taxes should be lowered, if possible. Current taxes should be used to cover costs, not to plug holes in the budget.

133743.explanation: Zaanstad has a participation ordinance that sets out how and when residents can have their say. If a village council is a beneficial addition, we are in favour.

133746.explanation: Absolutely. The POV has been advocating for this for years: a reasonable buy-out sum. Current home occupants must receive full clarity on the matter within the next four years.

133747.explanation: Yes and no. Industrial estates that offer suitable residential options can be modified accordingly. Other estates can be expanded and housing excluded, which gives clarity to both businesses and residents.

133748.explanation: Yes, and harsher measures are needed to punish environmental infringements. Cooperation with the regional environmental service also needs to be improved.

133749.explanation: Only where possible; the POV would prefer to build homes on the edges of the city that adjoin nature zones. If necessary, housing should be allowed to encroach on the edges of nature zones.

133750.explanation: No, they should not. But the national government must also let go of the housing quotas. Zaanstad residents must not be allowed to end up at the back of the line.

133751.explanation: No, absolutely not. Zaanstad must urge the national government to pursue other measures to reduce the numbers of incoming refugees. Zaanstad needs to tell The Hague that the maximum period for shelter is just that – a maximum.

133752.explanation: We are still not sure whether this is the cheapest option for residents. We support a cost differentiation per household. It is lunacy that a single person pays the same as a household of four.

133753.explanation: Disagree, although we do believe that sustainable business is important. Under the criterion specified here, businesses will not be willing to move to Zaanstad.

133754.explanation: Solar panels cover up the ground beneath them, so nothing else can grow. We need a solution that does no harm to nature.

133756.explanation: Disagree, simply because it is an unrealistic target.

133757.explanation: Disagree, electricity will always be needed as gas prices have risen sharply again recently. Solid agreements are needed on the feasibility of a district heating network, however.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines run on subsidies. Many investors and energy companies lose interest, due to high costs and poor returns. There should be no new turbines, and the old ones should not be replaced.

133759.explanation: Absolutely. An effective solution is needed to modify the corridor so it can be underground.

133760.explanation: Schiphol is a major regional employer. The national government has still provided no alternative. On the other hand, silent planes are becoming more and more common, which will lower noise pollution levels.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs should receive support once large investments are necessary. These clubs run on member contributions, and are not municipal property. It is important to help keep them running, however.

133763.explanation: Yes, absolutely agree. During the previous term, we have managed to retain the Omzoom for the neighbourhood until 2028. Alas, it is temporary, and should be made permanent. We must also look at how many events the area can support.

133764.explanation: Agreed. These should be created where possible: residents propose initiatives, which the municipality can then facilitate. This will prevent unchecked development.

133765.explanation: The municipality can give support where possible, but it is not the backer of sports associations. Together, we must look at where the possibilities lie. Clubs charge membership fees, and should be able to support themselves.

133766.explanation: No, absolutely not. Only if a building can be clearly shown to deserve monument status should it be allocated. Do not forget: heritage sites are extremely expensive to maintain.

133767.explanation: Agreed. Zaanstad is growing, which means that enforcement should grow accordingly. All kinds of regulations were thought up in the past, without taking into account the extra manpower needed for monitoring, etc.

133768.explanation: Agreed, where necessary and where it would provide a benefit. Many call for increased safety in Zaanstad, but few are willing to invest. Zaanstad should be safe for everybody.

133770.explanation: Absolutely, and schools are already doing so. We need to invest in projects that are currently running, and not give up. ‘Aanpak Veiligheid,’ the safety programme, will take time.

133771.explanation: Absolutely. The youth support work in Poelenburg last New Year's Eve showed just how important good youth work is. It needs broad support throughout Zaanstad.

133772.explanation: Disagree! There is a housing shortage, which means we must continue building. We do, however, need to take a closer look at where we build, and how to create access to new developments. The same applies to the parking problem.

133773.explanation: Absolutely. The solution to the housing bottleneck lies in providing mid-range rental housing starting at EUR 900. More social housing is not the answer. We must also build more homes – including owner-occupied – for the elderly who wish to downsize.

133774.explanation: We absolutely disagree here, this should be the responsibility of residents. The coddling must stop.

133775.explanation: Without demolition, there can be no renewal. Demolition would permit large numbers of new homes in a housing crisis.

133776.explanation: This is unattainable. We do, however, need to gain a clearer picture of where these facilities are needed, and act accordingly. We must look ahead, not wait until problems emerge.

133777.explanation: Zaanstad does this already. A permit is required before a house can be subdivided, in order to prevent nuisance resulting from room rentals. If possible, we must grant the permit.

133778.explanation: Agreed. Zaanstad must not become a dumping ground for the Amsterdam metropolitan region. Surrounding municipalities must also build more than they currently do, and otherwise we must leave WoningNet.

133779.explanation: Strongly disagree. 30% is enough. What we need are mid-range rentals to promote mobility from social housing.

133780.explanation: Agreed. This rule should apply to all of Zaanstad, with subletting only possible to family members. This type of rental should be licensed.

133781.explanation: There are several industrial estates where this is not possible, which is important for heavy industry and must remain so.

133782.explanation: Easing pressure on the inner city means moving to the edges of neighbourhoods. We need a careful look at how many homes are possible – but we must make them possible. The municipality also has a facilitating role in this respect.

133783.explanation: Yes, otherwise the efforts from previous years will have been for nothing.

133784.explanation: Absolutely. We have little more to add, except where it is not possible for physical or mental reasons.

133785.explanation: The current Zaanbrug bridge cost 32 million, and it works. We do not need yet another bridge across the Zaan!

133786.explanation: The decision has already been made to open the bridge, and it must not be reversed. It needs to open in any case, as soon as possible once the safety measures in Westerkoog are complete.

133787.explanation: This will compromise access to all villages and towns in Zaanstad. We should even consider creating more roads, to ensure good traffic flow.

133788.explanation: If possible this is our preference, but the cost is enormous.

133790.explanation: Absolutely not, these are high costs for little benefit. The money would be better spent on a good bus line that runs every night.

133791.explanation: Yes, absolutely agree! The A8/A9 link is an absolute must. It will also benefit the northern tip of North Holland, as well as future housing developments.

133792.explanation: Absolutely, an exit should be added on Thorbeckeweg to provide direct access to the neighbourhood. It will ease pressure on the inner city, and prevent congestion at Vijfhoek.

133793.explanation: No, absolutely not! Parking needs to be resolved as part of housing developments, which is entirely feasible in the city and also provides clarity to developers.

133794.explanation: Agreed. The agreement is that taxes should be used to cover costs, and not to patch up holes in the budget. Where possible, rates should therefore drop.

133795.explanation: This is undesirable in our view. Residents and businesses alike will incur extra costs, and the business climate will be severely impacted.

133796.explanation: Speed limits are already legally determined nationally. We do not need to add extra municipal regulations.

133797.explanation: Only if there is a clear benefit. Saendelft also had a nice hangout spot, which has since been demolished. Youth support work would be a better investment.

133798.explanation: There should be a community centre in every neighbourhood. Thanks to the POV, 19 community centres have been maintained over the last four years, without any budget cuts. A new community centre scheme is on the way, that will enable further expansion.

133799.explanation: People benefit more from respite care than money.

133800.explanation: Having children remains an individual responsibility, ridiculous proposition.

133801.explanation: The POV has supported free swimming lessons since 2010. With so much water in Zaanstad, children must be able to swim.

133741.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that every four years, residents should be able to elect those who represent them and make municipal decisions on their behalf.

133742.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that homeowners pay enough already, so if the OZB can be lowered, it should be lowered.

133743.explanation: De Zaanse VVD sees the importance of resident participation. Everybody should have a say, but not everybody should have decision-making power.

133746.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that it should be possible to buy out ground lease for a fair price.

133747.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes people should be able to live, work, and enjoy life in the municipality. Industrial estates are a necessary part of this mix.

133748.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that everybody should keep to the rules – businesses included.

133749.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes Zaanstad should be a place where people can live, work, and enjoy life. Industrial estates are therefore a necessity, and putting them at the edges of the municipality is a good option.

133750.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that young people in Zaanstad deserve priority for rental social housing.

133751.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that Zaanstad already does more than enough; exceeding the statutory amount will adversely affect our own residents.

133752.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that waste collection is a core municipal responsibility, and should not be pay-per-use.

133753.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that all businesses that contribute to Zaanstad are welcome, even though some are not yet in a position to work in a fully sustainable manner.

133754.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that the power shortage needs to be addressed as quickly as possible. If this helps, then we are in support.

133756.explanation: Zaanstad already does more than enough to fulfil the climate agreement. Increasing efforts will disadvantage our residents, both financially and in other ways.

133757.explanation: If feasible then yes, if not, we must not commit ourselves to a fixed date.

133758.explanation: Many wind turbines are already being built, that is all the available space there is.

133759.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that the power shortage needs to be addressed as quickly as possible. Unfortunately, these kinds of measures are unavoidable.

133760.explanation: Where night flights can be eliminated, they must be eliminated. In practice, most night flights are freight and holiday flights. These can easily be shifted to the daytime.

133761.explanation: Sport is extremely important, we must not let it go in Zaanstad.

133763.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that people should be a place where people can live, work, and enjoy life. An events site is a fitting addition.

133764.explanation: Sport is extremely important, and outdoor sports help facilitate community connection. We must not let it go in Zaanstad.

133766.explanation: People need to live in Zaanstad! Although some buildings are worth the trouble, it would be better for many to make room for new builds, and homes that meet the needs of 2026.

133767.explanation: Safety is crucial to feel comfortable in one’s own municipality.

133768.explanation: Cameras often lead to a greater sense of safety, because they are genuinely used to apprehend perpetrators!

133770.explanation: The municipality should seize weapons, and fine these children’s parents.

133771.explanation: All nuisance must be dealt with.

133772.explanation: In Zaanstad, we need to build for our residents.

133773.explanation: Everybody needs homes, this group is no exception. Building these kinds of homes promotes housing mobility, freeing up social rental housing and reducing the need to build more such homes, as Zaanstad already has a high number of social rentals.

133774.explanation: Everybody should be free to design their garden however they like.

133775.explanation: Where plans help improve the public realm and buildings are not of historic value, then demolition/new build must certainly be an option. Often this represents an opportunity for Zaanstad to meet the requirements of 2026.

133776.explanation: A pleasant neighbourhood is more than just houses.

133777.explanation: Subdivision has been proven to cause problems such as illegal accommodation of migrant workers and parking issues, since the street was not made to accommodate so many vehicles.

133778.explanation: As a rule, Zaanstad should build for its own residents.

133779.explanation: There are enough social rentals in Zaanstad; construction of mid-range rental and owner-occupied homes is the solution to the long social waiting lists.

133780.explanation: Buying a home as an investment in one’s old age or for one’s children should be perfectly possible. But slumlords who buy homes and rent them out in poor condition by the dozen should absolutely be dealt with, as they disrupt the housing market.

133781.explanation: If combining living and working is a valid option, then it should be possible to do so. Our industry must not be affected, however.

133782.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes people should be able to live, work, and enjoy life in the municipality. Because we need more than just apartments in Zaanstad, building on the edges of the municipality is a good option.

133783.explanation: The municipality must make better use of the budget, which never gets used up, year after year.

133784.explanation: De Zaanse VVD believes that working is the norm. Welfare is to cover temporary setbacks, and the municipality should be able to ask for something in return.

133785.explanation: The Zaanstad region is becoming congested, an additional bridge will help.

133786.explanation: When the Westerwatering was built, residents were promised that the bus bridge would be opened. It cannot open soon enough.

133787.explanation: Zaanstad is growing and so is the number of cars, so more roads are extremely important.

133788.explanation: Research has shown that this is technically impossible.

133790.explanation: Research has shown that this will cost €30 per passenger in subsidies!

133791.explanation: This connection is extremely important to our residents and for accessibility.

133792.explanation: Rapid travel from one side of the municipality to the other across a bridge that already exists is a good idea.

133793.explanation: There is an acute shortage of parking spaces across all neighbourhoods in Zaanstad.

133794.explanation: Paid parking should only be used to cover costs. De Zaanse VVD does not believe the municipality should profit from it.

133795.explanation: Stocking shops and restaurants is more important.

133796.explanation: Only where necessary for traffic safety.

133797.explanation: Meeting places have been proven to reduce disruptive behaviour.

133798.explanation: This is a nice idea, but financially untenable.

133799.explanation: An increase from €50 to €140 does not reflect our appreciation for the inestimable value of informal carers.

133800.explanation: Zaanstad residents expecting a child can access information through various other channels.

133801.explanation: We see the value of sport and exercise, but believe that responsibility for children learning to swim lies primarily with the parents.

133741.explanation: Agreed. Democracy does not mean voting once every four years: it is an ongoing process of public representation.

133742.explanation: Agreed. Municipal taxes cannot continue to rise to plug up financial leaks. Other measures are necessary, such as a critical evaluation of municipal spending patterns.

133743.explanation: Agreed. Abolishing the neighbourhood and village councils has pushed government and residents further apart. They should be reinstated.

133746.explanation: Agreed. The current system is demonstrably opaque, and its introduction has greatly harmed those involved.

133747.explanation: Disagree. There must be a clear boundary between housing and employment and their distribution, without them getting in each other’s way. Like things used to be, in other words.

133748.explanation: Disagree. There is no evidence that more monitoring is necessary than currently occurs via permit allocation, the regional environmental service, or regular enforcement.

133749.explanation: Disagree. There must be a clear boundary between housing and employment and their distribution, without them getting in each other’s way. Like things used to be, in other words.

133750.explanation: Disagree. Genuine social participation also means accepting the rules that apply to everybody. Besides, positive discrimination is still discrimination.

133751.explanation: Agreed. Zaanstad has enough social challenges among its own residents.

133752.explanation: Disagree. The municipality should look into waste-processing alternatives that are more efficient and cheaper in practice, such as post-sorting.

133753.explanation: Disagree. The definition of ‘sustainable’ is also arbitrary, as we have seen with biomass.

133754.explanation: Disagree. Solar panels ruin the landscape, and restrict agricultural businesses in their operations.

133756.explanation: Disagree. There is no need for Zaanstad to ever be climate neutral. Zaanstad's contribution to this issue globally is negligible.

133757.explanation: Disagree. Particularly in light of the imminent energy crisis, available alternatives can only be a good thing.

133758.explanation: Disagree. Wind turbines ruin the landscape, and run on subsidies.

133759.explanation: Agreed. The pylons ruin the landscape, and restrict agricultural businesses in their operations. There are also issues surrounding the health of residents living in relatively close proximity to the pylons, which will have an impact on future housing construction.

133760.explanation: Agreed. There is nothing wrong with calling attention to disturbance that continues to plague some of our residents.

133761.explanation: Agreed. Sports clubs are important for the health and well-being of our residents, and we see them as a municipal responsibility, which includes funding.

133763.explanation: Disagree. Given all the other spatial challenges in our municipality, this is unrealistic, and would be a waste of space besides.

133764.explanation: Agreed. Public spaces could be given a more lively and multifunctional purpose, which also benefits public safety.

133765.explanation: Agreed. As a core task, sport deserves funding, particularly to avoid forced relocations and mergers and help maintain its local character.

133766.explanation: Disagree. Monument status is not an end in itself, but an instrument for preserving that which has important cultural and historical value.

133767.explanation: Agree, though it is not so that more BOAs increase safety, at least not on their own. Additional measures are also necessary.

133768.explanation: Disagree. To us, preserving citizens’ freedom is just as important as ensuring safety. Besides, CCTV only increases safety if it is actively monitored, which is an expensive enterprise.

133770.explanation: Agreed. Though this is not just the municipality's job: it is also the responsibility of care providers and security services. Ultimately the problem is society-wide.

133771.explanation: Disagree. Zaandam-Oost is already spending and doing a lot, and the results must first become apparent before we know what works and what does not. There are also plenty of other neighbourhoods grappling with serious youth disturbance.

133772.explanation: Agreed. Urban densification should be examined for each area throughout the municipality, to evaluate what is realistic. The development plans for 20,000 new homes should also be re-examined in terms of local facilities and the protection of industry in Zaanstad.

133773.explanation: Disagree. Zaanstad has a relatively high proportion of low-income earners, which places additional pressure on social services. A better balance of financial capacity among residents would be an improvement.

133774.explanation: Disagree. This is not the municipality's job, and plenty is already being done to actively highlight the advantages of greener gardens. Some level of greening could also be imposed through changes to the municipal rules (APV) without extra cost to the municipality.

133775.explanation: Agreed, provided we do not lose sight of buildings genuinely deserving of preservation or monumental status. The municipality could assist by creating legislation or granting subsidies.

133776.explanation: Agreed. These were the norm in the 1960s and 70s, and create a greater sense of community in local neighbourhoods, as well as more activity and less nuisance through increased community self-monitoring.

133777.explanation: Agreed. Subdivision into rooms often creates additional nuisance in local neighbourhoods.

133778.explanation: Agreed. Most additional housing is currently being built for people from outside the municipality, and Zaanstad is shouldering a great deal of the regional demand.

133779.explanation: Disagree. Zaanstad has a relatively high proportion of low-income earners, which places additional pressure on social services. A better balance of financial capacity among residents would be an improvement.

133780.explanation: Disagree. The government should have no say in this matter.

133781.explanation: Disagree, unless the businesses in question are subject to different standards, or the homes have more than a purely residential purpose – such as combined living and supervision of the industrial sites.

133782.explanation: Disagree, it is important to protect our peat meadow areas.

133783.explanation: Disagree, there is no evidence to support this, and it is a major additional expense for which the municipality is not compensated.

133784.explanation: Agreed. These benefits are a safety net, and are intended as a temporary measure for when people are unable to manage on their own. Mutual participation is a useful step on the way to new work, to gain work experience, or to make a contribution to society.

133785.explanation: Agreed. There needs to be alignment with the Willis plan in Krommenie.

133786.explanation: Agreed, considering the environmental impact that a detour entails.

133787.explanation: Disagree. Various aspects of infrastructure in Zaanstad show room for improvement, and car numbers will not decrease as resident numbers grow.

133788.explanation: Agreed. We are in favour of the Zaanlijn train line being partly underground. It will improve traffic safety, free up land for nature and homes in Zaanstad, and reduce noise pollution.

133790.explanation: Agreed, that too should be a part of a growing, relatively large city.

133791.explanation: Agreed, although the project has already been cancelled once and for all. It is now time to search for alternatives.

133792.explanation: Agreed, this should improve traffic flows in Zaandam-Zuid considerably, and will prevent detours.

133793.explanation: Disagree. Creating fewer parking spaces is unrealistic – cars don't simply vanish into thin air.

133794.explanation: Agreed. Limited parking facilities create scarcity, which is also punished with high parking fees.

133795.explanation: Disagree. A low-emissions zone will have very adverse effects on business operations.

133796.explanation: Disagree. Lower speeds do not automatically equate to fewer accidents. The impact depends greatly on enforcement, road layout, and behaviour.

133797.explanation: Agreed, although this does depend on the makeup of the villages and the needs within those villages. It is debatable whether a youth community centre is always the solution. A meeting spot is.

133798.explanation: Agreed, if there is a genuine need. The idea behind this housing model could be beneficial, especially in light of increasing life expectancy.

133799.explanation: Disagree. An allowance is not the same as a subsidy, which is meant to incentivise specific behavior or efforts. Informal care is different, because people don’t choose it – they are simply faced with it.

133800.explanation: Disagree. A certain degree of independence can be expected of residents, which is generally also present. Various kinds of help and support are available for those who need it.

133801.explanation: Agreed. It should be part of schooling, as it was in the past.

133741.explanation: There are so many important decisions, it would be too costly and complicated for the council to organise a referendum every time. In ROSA’s view, residents should always be able to request a referendum, provided it is not commercial or misleading in nature.

133742.explanation: The OZB funds many important facilities for residents. A reduction would mean cutting costs in other areas. Housing costs in Zaanstad are around the national average. ROSA is satisfied with that.

133743.explanation: ROSA has always advocated for neighbourhood and village councils, who can consult on decisions affecting their local areas. The municipality should support them where necessary.

133746.explanation: ROSA supports a long-term lease system to combat property speculation. But if residents do wish to purchase the land under their homes, it should be handled in a fair and responsible manner. If there is an inexpensive option, so much the better.

133747.explanation: We need smarter use of existing industrial estates, for example through dual use or merging functions.

133748.explanation: The regional environmental service (Omgevingsdienst) carries out environmental checks for the municipality. We place great value on regular and effective monitoring!

133749.explanation: No, certainly not. The environment in Zaanstad is already under too much pressure.

133750.explanation: Unlike many others, recognised refugees have no social safety net, and should therefore remain eligible for priority, just like so many others in difficulty.

133751.explanation: While there are still Dutch municipalities that do not accommodate refugees, the remaining municipalities will need to do a little extra. ROSA is proud that Zaanstad is stepping up in this regard.

133752.explanation: Zaanstad still produces far too much landfill. To reduce it, producers will need to modify their packaging, and there should be financial incentives for residents who do their best to separate waste.

133753.explanation: In ROSA’s view, businesses who do more for sustainability than the statutory minimum are especially welcome.

133754.explanation: Yes, but let us start by filling up all the rooftops.

133756.explanation: No matter the agreements, we should all be doing our utmost to be climate-neutral as soon as possible. Incidentally, the original target was 2020...

133757.explanation: We need to phase out gas as soon as possible, both for the environment and our wallets.

133758.explanation: We need to make use of all sustainable energy sources. We would also like to see room for more traditional windmills, such as those at De Hemmes.

133759.explanation: Zaanstad has voiced its objection to this plan loud and clear. Unfortunately the province approved it, and the decision now lies with the national government.

133760.explanation: Alas, Schiphol is so powerful it seems to be above the law. Zaanstad should, of course, continue to apply as much pressure as possible.

133761.explanation: Society is showing a trend towards more individual sports, but team sports are also important, especially for children. So where sports associations still have a chance, we ought to help them where necessary.

133763.explanation: It should be possible to organise events throughout Zaanstad. The environment and other amenities must not suffer, however, and so multi-purpose venues (in multiple locations) are preferable.

133764.explanation: More and more people are choosing individual sports, which require local facilities.

133765.explanation: Thankfully, we already do so generously.

133766.explanation: Monument status is always a means, not an end. ROSA wants to retain Zaanstad's identity by continuing to tell its story. Allocating objects – which do not need to be old by any means –  monument and landmark status can help in this regard.

133767.explanation: We would prefer people to cameras on the streets.

133768.explanation: We would prefer people to cameras on the streets. Cameras give the illusion of security.

133770.explanation: Young people are under a lot of peer (and other) pressure, and it is important to do something about it. Thankfully Zaanstad already does this in various ways.

133771.explanation: This is already a budget focus. ROSA would prefer preventive measures: they cost far less than enforcement, and also help the young people themselves.

133772.explanation: ROSA has never wanted to build more than is necessary. Only the (distant) future can say what this means for Noord. Rigid figures are unwise at this early stage.

133773.explanation: The greatest demand is still for affordable homes. ROSA believes that the percentage of rental social housing in new developments could go up from 30 to 40 per cent.

133774.explanation: ROSA submitted a motion to this effect years ago. We would like to see it adopted as soon as possible!

133775.explanation: No more demolition! We should retain as many buildings as possible, both from both a sustainability standpoint and to maintain Zaanstad’s identity. Nowadays, it is also cheaper than demolishing and starting anew, and also provides architects and developers with exciting new challenges.

133776.explanation: A home is more than just a house: it is a social environment. Amenities and greenery are both important elements.

133777.explanation: Subdivision must comply with strict regulations, and is not desirable everywhere. However, adding more rooms can help people, such as students and other young people. Thankfully Zaanstad treats this issue with care.

133778.explanation: Yes, but without infringing on the rights of other urgent home seekers.

133779.explanation: Affordable housing is in great demand.

133780.explanation: To prevent property speculation, mandatory occupancy should apply where possible. Thankfully this is already the case for the more affordable homes in Zaanstad, but could be applied more widely in our view.

133781.explanation: Only if possible in terms of environmental impact.

133782.explanation: Don't build outwards – build upwards. We must preserve our green spaces for the environment.

133783.explanation: Incomes are The Hague’s jurisdiction. However, Zaanstad can help local residents on low incomes in all sorts of ways, which thankfully we do. Of course, we should always strive to do more.

133784.explanation: This is already the case, and is regulated by law.

133785.explanation: Zaanstad should not be enabling cars any further. We do support ferry services, which are enjoyable for pedestrians and cyclists.

133786.explanation: We have always objected to this. Where too many cars are the problem, we must not continue to encourage car use. Unfortunately, in a majority vote years ago, the council decided that the Busbrug bridge should be permanently opened. Parties’ opinions are now irrelevant.

133787.explanation: To promote freedom of choice, over the next few decades we should concentrate on provisions for pedestrians and cyclists, and public transport.

133788.explanation: A tunnel would avoid complicated level crossings, and create space for nature and homes. The resulting increase in train capacity would also relieve Zaanstad Noord of much road traffic.

133790.explanation: A proper night train would be very nice indeed.

133791.explanation: ROSA has always strongly opposed this devastating development.  We have consistently stood for the uncompromised protection of nature and the UNESCO World Heritage site, the Stelling of Amsterdam.  We were also against the sharp increase in car traffic through Zaanstad (at least 30,000 extra movements per day), and are therefore glad that the plan has been definitively scrapped.

133792.explanation: ROSA does not see the benefits of this plan.

133793.explanation: Yes, and mainly in heavily paved areas in need of more plants and play areas for children.

133794.explanation: Our parking fees are not high compared to other places. ROSA is also in favour of encouraging people to choose other modes of transport.

133795.explanation: Yes, because it significantly improves air quality, and reduces the greenhouse effect.

133796.explanation: A lower speed limit is better for safety, the environment, and liveability in general. A handful of through routes could potentially remain at 50 km/h.

133797.explanation: Young people need a ‘home away from home’ where they can come together safely.

133798.explanation: A community care centre is a nice middle ground between independent living and a nursing home. It allows partners to stay together, even when only one of them needs care. ROSA is in favour of small-scale residential facilities at neighbourhood level.

133799.explanation: Most informal carers appreciate support and temporary respite more than money. It would be better to spend the informal care budget on these aspects.

133800.explanation: ROSA believes it should be part of basic insurance. While that is not the case, it should be subsidised, especially for those with limited means.

133801.explanation: Swimming lessons are essential in an environment with so much water. Not all parents are in a position to organise it for their children, and so the municipality should step in. One option is to reintroduce school swimming lessons.

133741.explanation: The PVV supports referendums, but only for genuinely critical and far-reaching decisions. Zaanstad, however, only has the advisory referendum: an instrument without any binding influence, that the executive and council can simply ignore if they choose.

133742.explanation: The PVV wants the OZB to be as low as possible for its residents. Vacancy allows speculators to avoid paying the user portion of property tax, so that other residents and businesses pay a disproportionate amount. That is unfair.

133743.explanation: Citizen participation is useful, but has its limits. Council members are elected to make choices and show responsibility, not to avoid it.

133746.explanation: Ground lease in Zaanstad is unfair. Homes on ground lease are assigned the same assessed property value (WOZ) as homes on private land, meaning that ground lessees end up paying extra. The PVV wants to make ground lease fair and affordable again.

133747.explanation: Zaanstad essentially has enough industrial estates, however they are under threat from housing developments. If they disappear, they should always be replaced with new estates.

133748.explanation: Even though the municipality has administrative responsibility in this regard, it is the task of the regional environmental service (Omgevingsdienst).

133749.explanation: Industry is important in a growing city, however we must be selective about how to put it into practice. Nature must never form an obstacle to growth and livelihood security.

133750.explanation: Absolutely not, discriminating against our own residents in favour of recognised refugees is unacceptable. Our residents must receive local priority.

133751.explanation: Under the Distribution Act, we are required to accommodate around 800 asylum seekers, but we are currently hosting twice that number. That means double the problems. We are against the Distribution Act, but will continue to advocate for Zaanstad’s interests.

133752.explanation: We are already seeing a lot of illegal dumping, and this change will only exacerbate the problem. We do support a differentiated rate for single-person homes, however.

133753.explanation: What will it be next – that only sustainable residents are allowed to move here?

133754.explanation: The PVV is sceptical of weather-dependent energy sources (such as solar and wind), and is strongly opposed to their use on agricultural land. It is detrimental to both food production and the beautiful Dutch landscape.

133756.explanation: The Zaan Climate Agreement belongs in the shredder.

133757.explanation: The moral imperative in this statement already says it all; we support freedom of choice.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines in densely-populated areas are highly intrusive.

133759.explanation: We are against high-voltage power lines, and would prefer underground cables. If the province says it can’t be done, they must clearly demonstrate and support their stance.

133760.explanation: A flight ban applies to most commercial Schiphol flights from 11 p.m. to 6 a.m., with several exceptions, such as cargo or emergency flights.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs in Zaanstad receive subsidies for their accommodation, youth programs, and maintenance. Effective administration is needed for sports associations to remain financially healthy.

133763.explanation: An event site in Zaanstad-Noord is necessary, and could potentially be multi-purpose.

133764.explanation: These locations are little-used in practice, and often serve as youth hangout spots. it is better to make them multi-purpose.

133765.explanation: The municipality is responsible for sports facilities and clubhouses, and must make improvements where necessary.

133766.explanation: Not all old buildings are monuments.

133767.explanation: Many people no longer feel safe on the streets. The municipality should do all it can to improve its residents’ sense of safety.

133768.explanation: Many people no longer feel safe on the streets. The municipality should do all it can to improve its residents’ sense of safety.

133770.explanation: Education this, information that – it’s all talk. Carrying stabbing weapons is illegal, simple as that.

133771.explanation: Disruptive youth behaviour is a serious problem, and needs to be addressed. Enough regulations are in place; now we need adequate enforcement, because money alone will not solve the problem.

133772.explanation: While infrastructure fails to keep pace with the number of homes, no expansion is possible. Zaanstad Noord is becoming overcrowded and needs to retain its character; it now seems as though new construction is aimed at people from outside Zaanstad.

133773.explanation: More mid-range rentals will encourage mobility, and serve an oft-neglected demographic.

133774.explanation: Zaanstad does not even take proper care of its own public greenery, and should not get involved with other people’s.

133775.explanation: It should be possible to demolish a building that loses its functionality to make place for another, provided it does not have monument status and no speculation is at play.

133776.explanation: A growing city needs growing social amenities, such as doctors, childcare and schools.

133777.explanation: There are regulations to prevent subdivision, which must be strictly enforced. Too much of this in a street or neighbourhood disrupts the community and creates nuisance.

133778.explanation: Completely agree: the PVV wants priority to go to home seekers from Zaanstad and those with economic ties to the area, rather than too many homes going to recognised refugees.

133779.explanation: 30% rental social housing is more than enough for a balanced city; exceeding this level is financially unfeasible and disrupts the living environment, as in Poelenburg where over 60% of the homes are social housing.

133780.explanation: Price-hiking speculation must be avoided where there is a shortage. Owner-occupancy is therefore important.

133781.explanation: Industrial estates are full of industry. Building homes there will open the doors to complaints against the activities of the existing businesses.

133782.explanation: An extra road here and there is no problem, however village character should be preserved and the infrastructure must be able to cope.

133783.explanation: We are in favour of combating poverty, but see more value in effective support to prevent poverty and problem debts.

133784.explanation: Community service can be useful, but should not replace a paid job. Encouraging people to work is important, there are enough job vacancies. If no suitable work can be found, education or retraining is crucial, so that people can become financially self-sufficient.

133785.explanation: The only possibility the PVV can think of is an additional bridge at Wormer, which will do nothing for Zaanstad.

133786.explanation: This should have happened 30 years ago.

133787.explanation: A growing municipality needs infrastructure to keep pace.

133788.explanation: Putting the line underground is the best way to connect large sections of Zaanstad with each other, and to ensure proper access.

133790.explanation: It is ludicrous that a night train between Alkmaar and Amsterdam does not stop in Zaanstad. It would mean more passengers and income for the NS, and Zaanstad should certainly not have to bear the brunt of the costs.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 link is essential for accessibility, safety, and quality of life. PVV Zaanstad wants to do what is necessary, in the interests of our residents.

133792.explanation: Opening this bridge to cars between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder would funnel enormous amounts of cut-through traffic to Thorbeckeweg.

133793.explanation: Car ownership is still growing in Zaanstad, despite the municipality’s efforts to discourage it in recent years. Saddling residents with a parking problem is no solution.

133794.explanation: PVV Zaanstad supports a cheaper and fairer parking policy that makes sense. Parking should be a basic amenity, not a punitive measure.

133795.explanation: All planned low-emission zones should be scrapped: they damage the economy and limit accessibility.

133796.explanation: 30km is advisable in busy neighbourhoods, of course, but is clearly ridiculous on a main provincial road.

133797.explanation: Nearly every neighbourhood has a community centre or hall; these should also be available for use by young people.

133798.explanation: Nearly every neighbourhood has a community centre or hall; these should also be available for use by senior citizens.

133799.explanation: Informal carers are indispensable. Their allowance should go back up, without any ‘city pass’ nonsense. But respite care is perhaps just as necessary, to allow carers to provide better care without overtaxing themselves.

133801.explanation: We greatly value school swimming, but only for children attending school in Zaanstad.

133741.explanation: Residents should be able to have their say on important decisions.

133742.explanation: The property tax (OZB) should not be lowered. It is already relatively low in Zaanstad compared to other municipalities, and OZB revenue is needed for facilities such as healthcare, greenery, and culture.

133743.explanation: It is important to involve residents in municipal plans at an early stage.

133746.explanation: Zaanstad should give homeowners the option to buy out their ground lease. The price will vary from case to case.

133747.explanation: Zaanstad does not need any more industrial estates. The existing estates should remain accessible to businesses, however.

133748.explanation: Industry is important to the Zaanstad economy, but should not be to the detriment of liveability and the health of our residents. Pollution should therefore be prevented as much as possible.

133749.explanation: In addition to housing and employment, access to nature in the city is also important. Allowing industry to expand will be at the cost of housing, employment, or the environment.

133750.explanation: Just like other vulnerable groups, people who have received a residency permit deserve help finding a home.

133751.explanation: We believe that if people are sleeping outdoors in tents, Zaanstad should do its part to prevent it – even if it means taking on more asylum seekers than legally required.

133752.explanation: This policy could have undesirable effects, such as side-dumping or dumping outside municipal borders.

133753.explanation: We are in favour of an innovative economy, where businesses work sustainably. We find it important to make a clear choice in this regard, so that businesses wanting to move to Zaanstad know where they stand.

133754.explanation: Reaching the climate targets will require all forms of sustainable energy. We must consider dual use as much as possible, however.

133756.explanation: Setting the goal at 2040 is ambitious enough.

133757.explanation: Not all houses can phase out gas by this date. All new homes should be built gas-free, however.

133758.explanation: Reaching the climate targets will require all forms of sustainable energy. Wind turbines contribute a large amount, but they cannot be built everywhere. One area where we do still see possibilities is along the North Sea Channel (Noordzeekanaal).

133759.explanation: A rigorous procedure was followed to choose this location. D66 is committed to the outcome.

133760.explanation: Night flights cause a lot of disturbance.

133761.explanation: Nobody in Zaanstad should be excluded from sport.

133763.explanation: Many Zaanstad residents enjoy events and festivals. D66 believes more space should be available for them.

133764.explanation: Sport and exercise are beneficial to the health and welfare of our residents. More public space should be devoted to them.

133765.explanation: Sports associations should be enjoyable places for sports and recreation.

133766.explanation: We believe that protecting as many historic buildings as possible via monument status strengthens local identity, promotes sustainability and liveability, and prevents the loss of valuable heritage through short-term decision-making. Opportunities for future-oriented development must remain, however.

133767.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers must be deployed where they are needed most.

133768.explanation: Security cameras can increase security, but they also invade people’s privacy. The current policy offers sufficient balance.

133770.explanation: Unfortunately, we are seeing too many incidents involving stabbing weapons. Education can be of help.

133771.explanation: The disruptive behaviour in Zaandam Oost and Zaanstad Noord must be addressed. That requires more funding, which D66 would like to see invested in prevention.

133772.explanation: The need for housing is high, and so we see the construction of as many new homes as possible as a priority.

133773.explanation: We need a balanced mix of homes.

133774.explanation: Green gardens can contribute to climate adaptation, which D66 wants to encourage as much as possible.

133775.explanation: By demolishing old buildings and replacing them with housing, we can increase Zaanstad’s housing supply, which is urgently needed.

133776.explanation: For every new residential neighbourhood, we must ensure adequate amenities nearby such as schools, healthcare, shops, and sports & culture.

133777.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should combat subdivision of houses into rented rooms, as it puts strain on neighbourhood liveability and social cohesion. Tailored solutions are also needed: we must deal with illegal activity, but also ensure sufficient affordable housing alternatives so that shared living remains an option, not a necessity.

133778.explanation: We believe it is important for Zaanstad residents to continue to live in their own area if they choose.

133779.explanation: Disagree. More social housing means less other types of housing, such as mid-range rentals and homes for purchase. A good mix of housing is important.

133780.explanation: People should be able to decide what to do with their own homes.

133781.explanation: Industrial estates are needed for industry. There are other places in Zaanstad where homes can be built.

133782.explanation: D66 does not support building in nature areas, however at village borders it ought to be possible to a limited extent. D66 opposes construction in Natura 2000 areas.

133783.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should combat poverty, as financial stress limits opportunities and increases inequality. Rather than investing in poverty measures, however, we should instead invest in prevention, education, and sport.

133784.explanation: Everyone must be able to contribute meaningfully to society. This system also promotes access to paid work.

133785.explanation: D66 does not support any extra roads and/or bridges.

133786.explanation: This will promote smooth traffic flow and accessibility to Zaanstad West.

133787.explanation: D66 does not support any additional asphalt. Instead, we need investments in public transport and bicycle infrastructure.

133788.explanation: To improve the accessibility of Zaanstad by train, part of the line must run underground.

133790.explanation: Many Zaanstad residents enjoy the nightlife in Amsterdam, and it is important that they have a safe and sustainable way to get home.

133791.explanation: D66 does not support any additional asphalt, especially not through nature areas or UNESCO world heritage sites.

133792.explanation: D66 Zaanstad believes that the Vlinderbrug bridge should not be open to cars due to the negative effects on road safety, liveability, and sustainable mobility. The bridge is intended for safe use by bicycles and pedestrians, to promote a healthy, low-traffic municipality.

133793.explanation: D66 wants to reduce the amount of ground-level parking. This does not necessarily mean fewer parking spaces, however.

133794.explanation: People should be encouraged to travel by bicycle, public transport, or on foot.

133795.explanation: We approve the expansion of low-emission zones, as clean air is essential for health and liveability. A municipality-wide low-emission zone could help reduce emissions, subject to a socially fair implementation with effective transition regulations and support for residents and businesses.

133796.explanation: This is safer, and better for the environment.

133797.explanation: It is important for young people to have somewhere they can meet without causing any disturbance.

133799.explanation: Informal carers are very important, and deserve a token of appreciation.

133800.explanation: Equal opportunity for all starts at the cradle of every child.

133801.explanation: Disagree, we feel an income-based contribution is more appropriate.

133741.explanation: The CDA does not believe that referendums are an effective means of resident participation. They oversimplify complex choices, and often create more contradictions. Residents can provide more effective input through effective public consultation, and by providing feedback on plans that affect them.

133742.explanation: The CDA would like a lower OZB, but does not believe it is realistic. Less OZB means less funding for municipal care, facilities, and maintenance, to the detriment of residents who need these services daily. Lowering the tax is therefore not so simple.

133743.explanation: Resident participation is important, as residents know best what is happening in their area. However, clear agreements are needed on how the input from neighbourhood/village councils is to be incorporated into decisions.

133746.explanation: It is fair and important to offer homeowners clarity and choice when it comes to ground lease. Discounted purchase can be worthwhile for some, however this process is subject to all kinds of complications.

133747.explanation: It is important for Zaanstad to provide space for housing, exercise, recreation, greenery, and business. Expanding industrial estates would threaten this balance. Existing estates should be properly maintained, however, and remain appealing to businesses.

133748.explanation: Businesses should comply with regulations, and pollution is unacceptable. Still, it is important for monitoring to be proportional: enforcement where necessary, collaboration where possible, with consideration for both a healthy living environment and robust local industry.

133749.explanation: Zaanstad is a close-knit mix of industry, nature, business, and housing. To maintain liveability, nature must be preserved and given room to flourish.

133750.explanation: Recognised refugees must have the opportunity to integrate and need accommodation, however giving them consistent priority will increase pressure on social housing. We need a fair distribution, that takes into account both legal obligations and opportunities for other home-seekers.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad is working actively on a regional approach to asylum-seeker accommodation. We fulfil our regional responsibilities, and look beyond our own borders. We see the acceptance of more asylum seekers than necessary as a positive contribution.

133752.explanation: CDA Zaanstad does not believe that financial incentives will improve the waste disposal system. The risk is too great that residents who are unwilling or unable to pay will dump their waste illegally, creating more public clutter and inconvenience.

133753.explanation: The CDA sees the importance of increasing sustainability in industry, and believes we should give companies the chance to do so. Excluding companies at the outset is at odds with our business-friendly climate.

133754.explanation: Solar farms on agricultural land are not in keeping with Zaanstad's open character, and they damage the landscape. Recently, moreover, it has been shown that solar farms can cause interference for pilots (among others), resulting in serious safety concerns.

133756.explanation: The national and European goal is to be climate-neutral by 2050. Zaanstad is already 10 years ahead. We find this goal to be both appropriate and sufficiently ambitious.

133757.explanation: The CDA does not believe all houses should be gas-free by 2040. It is unrealistic, and such large investments are not feasible within so short a time. It does make sense for all new homes to be gas-free, however.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines affect the view, can impact health, and occupy a lot of space. Zaanstad already has enough wind turbines. We believe that small wind turbines for farmers’ personal use are appropriate, and would like to see more of them.

133759.explanation: It is preposterous that we can only advise on matters affecting our own municipal land. The proposed high-voltage power line corridor and accompanying transformer stations are detrimental to our open landscape, nature, the agrarian sector, and most of all, the health of our residents.

133760.explanation: Night flights create noise, and disturb the sleep of our residents. We were already promised that the disturbance would be reduced; now it is high time to put a stop to the night flights. Zaanstad should continue to pressure the national government on this matter.

133761.explanation: Sports associations are important to health, community, and social connection in our society. As a municipality, we bear the responsibility for a healthy and cohesive community, and we therefore support associations experiencing difficulty.

133763.explanation: We believe there should absolutely be space for events, but not necessarily a permanent location. Rotating sites have their own charm and can be multi-purpose, making them appealing and flexible.

133764.explanation: Outdoor sports are healthy, and contribute to vitality and social connection. Zaanstad already has several locations, however more facilities make sports more accessible to all and encourage an active, engaged community.

133765.explanation: Quality clubhouses and sports fields create good conditions for sports associations, and strengthen society. They offer space for both sports and other activities, providing added value for societies and residents alike.

133766.explanation: Our aim is to preserve the historic character of the Zaanstreek region. Monument status is not always a prerequisite; what is important is historic awareness, and careful consideration of proper preservation and responsible maintenance for each individual building.

133767.explanation: There is a shortage of enforcement officers and police on the streets. Security is suffering: we therefore need more enforcement and police officers to safeguard public safety in all its forms, and give residents a sense of security.

133768.explanation: Security cameras can help improve municipal safety, alongside prevention, more police on the streets, and better lighting. It is one way to improve safety, and give residents an increased sense of security.

133770.explanation: The municipality already provides information on the danger of stabbing weapons, which is important and effective. Prevention and proper education take time, but they do work in the long term and therefore remain essential.

133771.explanation: Youth disturbance is increasing in Zaandam Oost and Zaanstad Noord. The youth are our future, which is why we invest in providing guidance and opportunities. This approach strengthens a safe, united society, and helps get young people on the right track.

133772.explanation: The demand for affordable housing is high, for both seniors wanting to downsize and starters entering the market. More homes are essential to give everybody in Krommenie and Wormerveer a place where they feel at home.

133773.explanation: When planning new developments, a mix of low-cost, mid-range, and social housing is essential to serve all demographics and to strengthen the connections between towns and villages, giving everybody a place where they feel at home.

133774.explanation: There is already a free tile-collection service for people greening their gardens. Additional incentives are not necessary; residents need to see the value of greenery in their gardens themselves, and understand its importance for both the living and natural environment.

133775.explanation: Old or poorly maintained buildings sometimes need to be replaced with new ones. The CDA believes it is important for new buildings to suit the surrounding environment, and contribute to an attractive and liveable neighbourhood.

133776.explanation: Neighbourhood liveability needs certain essentials: schools, doctors, childcare, and – in our view – sporting associations. This helps residents quickly feel at home, and keeps neighbourhoods lively, connected, and appealing to everybody.

133777.explanation: Subdivision into rented rooms creates disturbance and poor living conditions. The CDA will continue to actively address this issue, to improve neighbourhood liveability and to ensure that housing is used fairly and responsibly.

133778.explanation: Rented social housing is primarily for Zaanstad residents who want to stay here. The CDA believes they should receive priority allocation, so that people can continue to live in their own neighbourhoods, strengthening the community.

133779.explanation: We believe the current level of 30% social rental housing is appropriate. We need room for a mix of housing, to provide for all target groups and keep Zaanstad liveable and appealing to everybody.

133780.explanation: Mandatory owner-occupation promotes liveability, and ensures a level playing field on the market. It discourages investors, and makes it easier for groups such as starters to purchase homes, as there is less investment competition on the market.

133781.explanation: Housing on industrial estates is not a quick fix, as industrial activity can hamper the quiet enjoyment of one’s home. The idea can be explored where possible, but the appeal and functionality of the relevant estates should remain prime considerations.

133782.explanation: The fringes of small towns often contain unused space that is suitable for small-scale development. The limited space requires a creative approach to construction without encroaching on nature zones, to preserve liveability and natural surroundings.

133783.explanation: Zaanstad already makes major investments to combat poverty. More important than the amount is the effective allocation of funds, to ensure that residents genuinely receive help and support that is tailored to their specific needs.

133784.explanation: Not everybody on benefit is able to perform community service or undertake study. It is welcome where possible, but an individual approach is necessary so that support remains appropriate and feasible for everybody.

133785.explanation: We currently do not see any need for an additional bridge across the Zaan. Effective maintenance of existing roads and connections is more important, and should be improved where possible to optimise traffic and accessibility.

133786.explanation: The De Binding bus bridge is already open 24 hours a day, which we applaud. This comprehensive access route improves Westwatering’s connection to the rest of Zaanstad, making travel for residents faster, safer, and easier.

133787.explanation: New homes need good access routes. Additional roads help prevent congestion and ensure smooth traffic flow, in turn addressing village cut-through traffic, and ensuring that accessibility and liveability go hand-in-hand.

133788.explanation: The Zaanlijn needs to be widened for trains to run more frequently. Tunnels can prevent crossing congestion and improve local conditions, but are dependent on sufficient resources and effective cooperation between all relevant parties.

133790.explanation: There is little demand for a night train between Zaanstad and Amsterdam. There is already a night bus, and CDA would prefer to improve this existing connection rather than invest in a night train.

133791.explanation: It is unacceptable that villages in the region are being used to absorb regional traffic congestion. The A8/A9 link is crucial to the safety and health of our residents. Zaanstad will therefore continue to urge provincial and national authorities to build it.

133792.explanation: Cars crossing the Vlinderbrug bridge would cause additional cut-through traffic, impacting safety and residents’ quality of life. Opening it up is therefore inadvisable; Poelenburg must remain a safe and pleasant residential area for residents.

133793.explanation: Many residential areas already have too few parking spaces. Fewer spaces will cause more problems due to illegal parking. The layout of new developments must be carefully considered, to ensure enough room for people to park their cars.

133794.explanation: Paid parking is still needed, to manage parking demand and allocate space fairly. Cheaper parking will not solve this problem, however parking policy must be clear, well explained, and consistently applied.
