133795.explanation: The current plan is to introduce three small low-emission zones in Zaanstad. The CDA has argued to postpone it, partly to help smaller businesses. A comprehensive municipal low-emission zone is excessive, unrealistic, and lacks community backing.

133796.explanation: Lower speeds in the built-up area help improve traffic safety. Reducing traffic speeds make streets safer for residents, cyclists, and children in our towns and villages.

133797.explanation: If young people need a place to meet, then there should be one – perhaps a sports club, or other facility. It does not always need to be a municipal responsibility; it is better if the ideas come from young people themselves, and the municipality facilitates.

133798.explanation: Quality care and community are important for seniors, but community care centres do not need to be mandatory in every village. Needs vary between neighbourhoods. Appropriate, accessible care should be built where necessary, close by where possible, and in conjunction with existing services.

133799.explanation: Informal carers are worth their weight in gold, but time and attention show appreciation more than money. The CDA therefore advocates for respite care, to reduce the burden on informal carers and give them much-needed breathing space.

133800.explanation: Free antenatal classes are often already available at the Municipal Public Health Service (GGD). The CDA believes it is important for existing and expectant parents to be referred to the GGD, and to receive supplementary courses and information for maximum support.

133801.explanation: Making swimming lessons completely free is not a municipal responsibility, and will put pressure on swimming pools. Swimming lessons should be accessible to all, and there are already support schemes available for those who cannot afford it. It is more important to reintroduce school swimming programmes for all children, to encourage swimming.

133741.explanation: Residents should be given a say in plans with major impact.

133742.explanation: For many people, housing is already expensive enough.

133743.explanation: Residents are aware of the issues affecting their neighbourhood or village.

133746.explanation: This will offer residents housing security.

133747.explanation: There is little space inside the city, and the space outside is needed for jobs.

133748.explanation: The health of residents is paramount.

133749.explanation: This is sometimes necessary for employment, provided there is due regard for nature and the surrounding environment.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad is willing to help people, while safeguarding local capacity and liveability.

133752.explanation: This will put more trash on the streets.

133753.explanation: Companies need to treat humans and the environment properly.

133756.explanation: The municipality should encourage, not impose. Residents should determine the pace.

133757.explanation: Only if it remains affordable – no extra requirements for people already doing enough.

133758.explanation: Only with the support of local residents.

133759.explanation: Protect nature and the health of residents.

133760.explanation: People have a right to sleep and peace and quiet.

133761.explanation: Sport is healthy and brings people together.

133767.explanation: Visible enforcement presence creates a sense of safety.

133768.explanation: Where required for safety, as a targeted measure.

133771.explanation: Investing in youth is a proven strategy.

133772.explanation: There is a great need for homes.

133773.explanation: Distribution varies between neighbourhoods – residents should be given a say.

133774.explanation: Encouragement is okay, obligations are not.

133775.explanation: New buildings are often more sustainable and better for health.

133776.explanation: This also helps against illegal activity and slum landlords.

133778.explanation: Lots of young people want to keep living in Zaanstad.

133781.explanation: Home and work can be combined, which reduces traffic.

133782.explanation: Necessary due to housing shortage, with retention of greenery.

133785.explanation: It's better to improve existing routes.

133786.explanation: This would create too much disturbance.

133787.explanation: Cars remain a necessity for many people.

133788.explanation: This would make neighbourhoods quieter and safer.

133791.explanation: This would improve liveability and accessibility.

133792.explanation: This comes from the neighbourhood itself, and will improve traffic and safety.

133793.explanation: We need more parking spaces, not fewer.

133794.explanation: It should be free for everybody.

133795.explanation: Too big an impact on people with low incomes.

133797.explanation: Young people need their own space.

133800.explanation: Good start for parents and child.

133741.explanation: We develop plans in conjunction with residents, via citizens’ assemblies and local consultation bodies. This leads to more well-informed decisions. For big issues, a well-organised referendum can help to generate positive, concrete solutions.

133742.explanation: More funding is required in the years ahead for healthy living environments, quality healthcare (including youth care), and more housing. National budget cuts have placed Zaanstad before major financial challenges. A lower property tax (OZB) will mean cancelling other important services.

133743.explanation: Citizen participation needs renewal: in addition to town/village councils, we also want neighbourhood referendums and citizens’ assemblies for big issues. Allocating neighbourhood budgets will give residents the opportunity to increase the liveability of their area for humans and animals.

133746.explanation: Land owned by Zaanstad remains under municipal control. In principle, the PvdD is therefore against buyouts, but does insist that ground lease be low for households, to avoid putting people in financial difficulty.

133747.explanation: With space at a premium, choices need to be made. We need more green outdoor spaces and nature zones, as well as more homes and more local facilities, such as community centres. the PvdD would rather invest in these things than in more industrial estates and more consumer goods. Sustainable businesses can be integrated as well as possible into existing estates.

133748.explanation: Enforcement is a high priority at the regional environmental service (Omgevingsdienst), as a clean living environment is essential to our health and to nature. Industry violations will lead to swifter fines or company closures.

133749.explanation: Many animal and plant species are dying out because we are polluting and fragmenting their habitats. Zaanstad is also home to species at risk of extinction. We must therefore do all we can to protect, restore, and expand nature areas.

133750.explanation: Recognised refugees receive priority because, just like others in difficulty, they need living space urgently. The real problem is the housing shortage: previous cabinets have stripped back housing associations, while the need for rental social housing has grown. Zaanstad must support and encourage these associations to build a great deal more housing.

133751.explanation: The national shortage of resources has put asylum seekers on the streets, and their procedures take too long. Every municipality should therefore do all it can. Zaanstad has committed to doing more than the norm, but we must continue to urge other municipalities to at least meet their obligations.

133752.explanation: It is fair for people and businesses who create more waste to pay more. We want to reward waste separation, to allow more recycling. More research is needed to determine whether a pay-per-use/pay-per-household (DIFTAR) system would work best.

133753.explanation: Zaanstad must influence which companies establish themselves here, deploying space in a targeted fashion for sustainable, social, and future-proof businesses. Support for local circular startups will generate more local employment and social cohesion.

133754.explanation: Agricultural land is not meant for solar farms, but for plant-based foods and environmental development, and – if livestock farming decreases significantly – for housing. Solar panels and greenery are becoming the norm for rooftops, façades, sound barriers, and on top of car parks.

133756.explanation: Government negligence has put climate goals in danger, despite the urgency and visible consequences, even at local level. Zaanstad must therefore become climate-neutral as soon as possible, setting the target at 2030.

133757.explanation: To be climate-neutral by 2030, Zaanstad must quickly phase out gas for cooking and heating, amenities that must remain affordable and come from genuinely sustainable heat sources. The municipality must ensure monitoring and targeted subsidies. Alternatives can be investigated for historic monuments, such as hydrogen cells.

133758.explanation: Zaanstad promotes wind energy in places where there is little to no impact on people, animals or nature, e.g. in the Noordzeekanaalhaven (North Sea Canal Port) or on industrial estates. We are against wind turbines in nature zones, or in areas with vulnerable bird or bat populations.

133759.explanation: A sustainable energy supply will require significant upgrades to the energy network. We are not against expanding the network per se, but the impact on humans, animals and nature must be kept to a minimum. Sustainable energy cannot be channelled into unnecessary industry.

133760.explanation: Schiphol is making people sick. People who live nearby deal with the negative effects daily, leading to mental and physical symptoms. The government should offer its residents protection. The PvdD therefore wants a direct halt to night flights, and a substantial reduction in Schiphol’s activities.

133761.explanation: Mergers and fewer sporting locations are making sports less accessible. Zaanstad needs to do more to keep sports clubs afloat. Where possible, support should go to specific targets, such as sports for children from low-income families and people with disabilities, and increasing the longevity of club buildings.

133763.explanation: The Omweg event site in Assendelft would make a good permanent location, as the impact on surrounding residents and nature is minimal. When looking at alternatives, we will therefore closely examine the effects for people, animals, and nature.

133764.explanation: The municipality designs public space so that everyone can play sport and exercise safely, including via clubs and associations. Sport must be accessible and inclusive, with facilities in every neighbourhood: from outdoor gyms and fitness corners to playing fields and skate parks.

133765.explanation: Zaanstad should invest in sports on real grass, and replace artificial turf with rubber granulate as quickly as possible. If necessary, we will also earmark funds to improve the longevity of clubhouses, and for investments to make sports more accessible.

133766.explanation: Protecting heritage is vital to our Zaanstad history and culture. But the municipality is changing: we also need space for sustainability, and new housing models and facilities. Monument status limits flexibility; we must therefore proceed with caution, and not try to apply it everywhere.

133767.explanation: Additional enforcement resources are needed to address environmental offences, animal abuse, illegal logging, street harassment and discrimination, and to safeguard peaceful protests. Investments in social services (e.g. night shelters, proper healthcare) will give BOAs more time for these tasks.

133768.explanation: For privacy reasons, great care must be taken with security cameras in public. Video surveillance gives only the appearance of safety, and often only shifts the problem elsewhere. Automatic facial recognition is too intrusive, and must not be used.

133770.explanation: Possession of weapons needs to be drastically reduced. Education is necessary, but not sufficient. Poverty and racism can also be at play: young people sometimes have nothing left to lose. Try to make contact with them, and organise places where they can meet. Invest in youth service workers.

133771.explanation: Via PACT Zaandam Oost, we are investing in support for youth, for children in poverty, and combating disruptive behaviour and crime. Noord, too, is devoting special attention to youth problems. We would prefer to allocate additional resources to prevention and talent development, for lasting and improved results.

133772.explanation: Zaanstad can create beautiful and green inner-city neighbourhoods. In Zaanstad-Noord (including Assendelft), facilities and public transport must keep pace with population growth. Ecological corridors should be a prerequisite; projects without them should not proceed.

133773.explanation: The rental social housing pool has shrunk, while many Zaanstad residents and young people have been waiting too long for an affordable home. The solution is building: more rental social housing, to reduce waiting times. Focusing exclusively on encouraging mobility towards home ownership and the free market is not enough.

133774.explanation: Green gardens provide space for animals and insects, offer shade, and retain rainwater. Zaanstad should therefore continue to collect tiles, and encourage greening via information and subsidies for initiatives such as vertical green, small front gardens, native plants, rainwater barrels, and green roofs.

133775.explanation: Renovation and redevelopment should have priority over demolition and new builds: they are more sustainable, and protect the animals living in and around older buildings. Landmark buildings, trees, avenues, and neighbourhoods must be preserved.

133776.explanation: Housing developments must always take social provisions into consideration, such as schools, greenery, care centres, public transport, parks, playgrounds, and shops. Zaanstad is 100% committed to the multifunctional use of space.

133777.explanation: Subdivision of homes can help to combat the housing shortage. It must be suitable for the neighbourhood, and not unduly impact public space or green areas. We must set clear regulations to avoid abuses.

133778.explanation: Higher priority for local home seekers sounds logical, but can be a net negative: if other municipalities do the same (such as Amsterdam), it will limit the available options for those in Zaanstad. We therefore argue both for a more balanced prioritisation policy, and for more rental social housing in the region.

133779.explanation: More affordable homes are on the way: 40% of all rental homes should be rental social housing, finally increasing the proportion of this kind of housing in Zaanstad over the next few years.

133780.explanation: We are in favour of measures to combat speculation and vacancy, such as mandatory occupation and a vacancy ordinance with sanctions. Homes are for living in, not making a profit.

133781.explanation: A healthy living environment is one where sound, smell, light, and air quality do not cause any problems, day or night. This cannot be guaranteed on many industrial estates in Zaanstad. As a rule, we are therefore against homes on industrial estates.

133782.explanation: Housing is a fundamental right. We want real solutions: tackling vacant buildings and repurposing them as homes; densifying where necessary, with amenities that keep pace with the population and nature-inclusive construction; and converting livestock farmland into nature areas, reserving a section for housing developments.

133783.explanation: Poverty is the result of rising inequality, and is made worse by discrimination. We want liveable incomes, with affordable rent and food. A universal basic income pilot, generous welfare, greater support for people without a roof over their heads, and simple anti-poverty schemes free from pointless monitoring.

133784.explanation: The Participation Act was a failure. We can see that forcing people to perform paid work is ineffective. All welfare recipients have their own story; the municipality must listen, and respond accordingly. Offering a training course can help, but we must also recognise the value of volunteer work or informal care.

133785.explanation: If this proves to be a genuine need, e.g. for improved public transport or cycling routes, or nature (ecological corridors), it can be investigated.

133786.explanation: Opening up the bus bridge during peak hour will make Westerkoog dangerously busy, at the cost of many old trees due to necessary ring-road modifications and increased nitrogen levels in Westzijderveld. None of this aids nature recovery. We should be discouraging car use, not enabling it.

133787.explanation: High-polluting traffic should be discouraged, and clean traffic promoted. This will make a major contribution to climate targets, to a healthier and more attractive municipality, allow for reclamation of green spaces in our streets, and improve safety for children and animals.

133788.explanation: Absolutely! Putting the Zaanlijn underground will allow more trains to run, as well as directly reconnect various neighbourhoods and create space for small parks and additional housing. Train-related noise pollution in Zaanstad will also decrease. Everybody benefits!

133790.explanation: A Zaanstad night train could improve convenience and passengers’ sense of safety. Zaanstad should issue funds to the NS, to allow night trains to make at least 2 stops in Zaanstad.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 connection will merely shift the problem, and cause more cars to travel through Koog aan de Zaan. For cleaner air in Zaanstad-Noord, we need the opposite: fewer polluting cars on the road. Rather than a costly road through a Natura 2000/world heritage site, we would rather have a low-emissions zone.

133792.explanation: This bridge is meant for public transport. Ultimately, a high-quality public transport link through Zaandam is dependent on a connection with Amsterdam. Opening the Vlinderbrug bridge to car traffic will create congestion, compromising the quality of public transport. That should not be our goal.

133793.explanation: We want a liveable municipality, with enough greenery, space for animals, and room for children to play. Where there is adequate parking, some spaces could be converted to small green areas or playgrounds. We would also like to add greenery to paved parking spaces, e.g. using half-open paving stones.

133794.explanation: Parking occupies a large amount of public space – space that is needed for plants and animals, and for recreational and meeting places. Encouraging alternative forms of transport, parking garages, and P&R will enable us to reduce the number of parking spaces. A parking tax will help in this regard.

133795.explanation: Of course! We must continue to reduce fossil fuel usage. By making all Zaanstad a low-emissions zone, we are taking a clear step towards a healthy and liveable future. The municipality will need to make continued efforts to aid the rapid transition to fossil-free forms of transport.

133796.explanation: Lower speeds reduce the likelihood of accidents, and also create a more pleasant living environment with less noise. Cycling, walking and public transport will become more attractive, and the extra space can be used for trees and shrubs to combat heat stress and flooding.

133797.explanation: Health has both a physical and mental component. Spending time together, discovering talents, exercising, and relaxation are all essential to the safe and healthy development of young people. A meeting place that speaks to them can help in this regard.

133798.explanation: Housing models such as community care centres allow people to both help each other and receive professional care. They can also share spaces and resources, which is good for the environment, the climate, and people’s bank accounts. It also combats loneliness and promotes social cohesion.

133799.explanation: Informal care can be a positive way to offer primary or additional care to family members, but is often also a great responsibility. Volunteer work is work, and should be rewarded. In the current system, this boils down to an ‘appreciation allowance.’ €50 is far too little, €140 is moving in the right direction.

133800.explanation: A good start is essential for children's development. Ante-natal classes can help in this regard. If expectant parents cannot afford them, Zaanstad should offer them for free.

133801.explanation: It is important for all children to learn to swim. If parents cannot afford lessons, Zaanstad must ensure that these children can get their swimming diploma for free.

133741.explanation: The SP even supports a replacing the advisory referendum with a binding corrective referendum, giving citizens a way to rein in local politicians.

133742.explanation: The SP’s only opinion of the OZB is that it should not be too low or too high.

133743.explanation: The SP wants more neighbourhood democracy. We should therefore reintroduce neighbourhood/village councils, where residents have a genuine say over what happens in their area.

133746.explanation: The SP believes that ground-lease conversions should be subject to implementation regulations, including annual parameters.

133747.explanation: The SP believes that more industrial estates will increase traffic density, which the green and open spaces around the city can no longer support. Zaanstad must change and grow within the built-up area.

133748.explanation: Zaanstad still has factories that do not comply with modern environmental standards, and even more industry that cannot meet the future requirements. Industry needs to rectify both of these situations in short order, and strict monitoring must be employed.

133749.explanation: Zaanstad is surrounded by no fewer than three European nature reserves, where the environment is strictly protected. Here in Zaanstad, we must take that into consideration. Even at the edges of these areas, nature must be protected. Industry has no place there.

133750.explanation: In 2023, 6.9% of the social housing units that became available in the Zaanstad region were allocated to recognised refugees. That is a small amount. Zaanstad can therefore continue prioritising social housing, including for recognised refugees.

133751.explanation: Asylum seekers housed in Zaanstad are not merely part of Zaanstad’s allocation, but are part of the regional distribution. There have been no signs of social disturbance. There is no reason why Zaanstad should not be able to accept more asylum seekers than nationally mandated.

133752.explanation: A pay-per-use system for underground waste containers (DIFTAR) paves the way for illegal and side-dumping, as people do not want to pay for their waste. It is also unfair to large families, and people with large amounts of medical waste. They have no choice.

133753.explanation: The SP believes that industry needs to clean up, and comply with current and future environmental standards in short order. There is no room for new polluters, and so it makes sense that only businesses with sustainable operations be permitted to move here.

133754.explanation: The SP feels that as many roofs as possible should be fitted with solar panels. This applies to homes and businesses alike. Agricultural land should be used for sustainable, non-polluting farms or new nature zones.

133756.explanation: The SP believes Zaanstad should be climate-neutral by 2040, or sooner if possible. The municipality should strive to meet this target.

133757.explanation: New houses should no longer be connected to the gas network, and it must be phased out of existing homes. The SP believes this should occur at no extra cost to the residents. Affordability for residents is important to the SP.

133758.explanation: The energy transition will require both solar and wind energy. Wind turbines cannot be placed everywhere, and the Zaanstad region is home to valuable nature zones. Zaanstad can accommodate more wind turbines, provided they are not placed in nature zones and cause no problems for the surrounding residents.

133759.explanation: The SP believes there should be no high-voltage power corridor in Assendelft and Westzaan.

133760.explanation: The night flights to and from Schiphol need to stop. And not only that: the SP believes Schiphol’s growth should be halted, and efforts made to reduce the number of flights to and from Schiphol in general.

133761.explanation: The SP believes that sports should be accessible and affordable to all, including people on low incomes, children, and seniors. The SP is in favour of municipal funding that makes this possible.

133763.explanation: Zaanstad Noord could have an event site where space allows and there is no disturbance to residents or impact on the environment.

133764.explanation: The SP believes there should be more public sporting facilities for young and old.

133765.explanation: The SP believes that sports should be accessible and affordable to all, including people on low incomes, children, and seniors. The SP is in favour of municipal funding that makes this possible. Of course fields and clubhouses must not be poorly maintained.

133766.explanation: Giving historic buildings monument status helps to preserve them.

133767.explanation: The SP believes that there are still too few police and enforcement officers in Zaanstad, and we would like to see both of their numbers increase. These additional uniforms should focus more on preventing criminal and unwanted behaviour than on punishment.

133768.explanation: The municipality already has extensive camera surveillance. Security cameras need to be effective, and not only create the illusion of security. The SP is also concerned about infringing the privacy of residents with too many cameras in public.

133770.explanation: Zaanstad already educates young people on the dangers of owning and using stabbing weapons. Parents, neighbours, coaches, and teachers are also asked to make the topic open for discussion, and encourage Zaanstad youth to do away with weapons.

133771.explanation: The municipality is already investing more to combat the problems in Saendelft, as well as in the collaboration to address youth issues in Zaanstad Noord and Zaandam-Oost. In the SP’s view, this is sufficient. Where there are incidents, these problems should be addressed.

133772.explanation: The need for housing in Zaanstad is high, which is why the planned 5000 homes in Krommenie and Wormerveer need to go ahead.

133773.explanation: The focus of new builds should be on rental social housing. The need for housing is most acute among those who qualify for social housing.

133774.explanation: The SP believes that it is reasonable to ask our residents to no longer fully pave their yards, but to plant at least two-thirds with flowers, shrubs, and trees. If the municipality can offer a financial incentive, that would help.

133775.explanation: Although renovation and increased sustainability should be the norm, sometimes it is better to wipe the slate clean and start again.

133776.explanation: The liveability of new neighbourhoods suffers if their residents must wait years for amenities such as public transport, schools, supermarkets, childcare, or doctors.

133777.explanation: The SP is against subdivision into rooms, but wants to encourage friends living together, shared housing, splitting homes, and lodger rentals — provided these are well coordinated and monitored. These measures help to combat the housing shortage and homelessness.

133778.explanation: The SP would like to add a side note: it is already possible to allocate 50% of rental social housing to home seekers within our own municipality. And in 2024, 73% of the available rental social housing went to Zaanstad residents. Local residents already have priority.

133779.explanation: There is a housing shortage in Zaanstad. The greatest need is among those who qualify for social housing. At least 40% of new builds should therefore be dedicated to rented public housing. In the SP’s view, the percentage of rental social housing should be even higher.

133780.explanation: The municipality must expand the reach of mandatory owner-occupancy, which will prevent homes from being bought up by investors who do not intend to live in them.

133781.explanation: Zaanstad will soon see new homes built on the Achterluispolder industrial estate. Where possible, the SP believes that housing is also possible on other industrial estates.

133782.explanation: To protect and preserve the environment and green areas in and around Zaanstad, we must build within established population centres, not at their edges.

133783.explanation: Many children (and parents) in Zaanstad are still living in poverty. Poverty has a far-reaching impact on people in society, including health problems, limited access to education, and social exclusion. The municipality should invest more to combat poverty.

133784.explanation: For people on welfare benefits, the focus should be on what they are willing and able to do, not on what they are required or permitted to do. Give freedom to people on social assistance, so they can continue to participate in society in the way they choose.

133785.explanation: It depends on where the extra bridge will go. Currently, Zaanstad and Wormerland are investigating the possibility of a third bridge across the Zaan between Wormerveer and Wormer. The SP will await the results of this study.

133786.explanation: The SP is against the 24-hour opening of the De Binding bus bridge as it is detrimental to the Polder Westzaan Natura 2000 area. It will also drastically increase car (and other) traffic on the Westerkoog ring road, necessitating expansion of the road and the removal of trees.

133787.explanation: The SP believes that the municipality should prioritise the expansion and improvement of the bicycle network and public transport. We also believe that no new roads should be built in or nearby Natura 2000 areas. The A8-A9 project must be abandoned once and for all, also by Zaanstad.

133788.explanation: Putting the Zaanlijn train underground broadens future opportunities. It will allow more homes to be built, and improve connections between neighbourhoods in the east and west of Zaanstad.

133790.explanation: There is an excellent night bus that runs between Amsterdam and Krommenie-Assendelft. The buses leave every hour, both from Amsterdam and from Krommenie. The night bus also stops at all stations in Zaanstad, with many additional stops on the way.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 connection will not go ahead. The reasons relate to the environment, heritage, traffic congestion elsewhere, and the great expense involved. Zaanstad should stop lobbying national and provincial government, and simply accept that the road is not a possibility.

133792.explanation: De Vlinder is intended to facilitate smooth bus transport. Opening it up to cars would be detrimental to public transport accessibility. It should therefore remain closed to car traffic.

133793.explanation: Through well-considered parking policy, the SP aims to reduce car numbers in Zaanstad’s neighbourhoods. The fewer parking spaces there are, the more homes can be built.

133794.explanation: We believe that parking fees could be reduced, provided this does not create more parking problems in certain streets and districts. If these problems get worse due to cheaper parking, then parking rates should not be lowered.

133795.explanation: The entire municipality of Zaanstad could become a low-emissions zone, if accompanied by a buyout scheme for old diesel vehicles.

133796.explanation: The maximum speed limit for nearly all roads within the built-up area should be 30 km/h. 30 km/h zones increase road safety, reduce noise pollution and contribute to air quality, all significant improvements to the liveability of the area. Travel time also barely increases as a result.

133797.explanation: The SP believes that every neighbourhood should have adequate youth facilities, which include meeting places. Every neighbourhood should also include enough playgrounds for children.

133798.explanation: The SP believes that every neighbourhood needs a community care centre for senior citizens. A community care centre is a small-scale facility where senior citizens can live and grow old in their own neighbourhood or village, where independence and a sense of community are the main focus.

133799.explanation: The main goal of informal carers is to provide care to those who need it. They do this from the goodness of their heart, but in so doing, they also save the municipality money. The ‘appreciation allowance’ that allows caregivers to feel recognised, could certainly be higher than is currently the case.

133800.explanation: The Zaanstreek-Waterland municipal health service (GGD) already offers antenatal and childbirth classes, which should be accessible to anybody expecting a child. If the classes are free, all pregnant people can attend, regardless of income.

133801.explanation: Swimming lessons are costly, and growing numbers of Dutch children have no swimming diploma. Children stop swimming lessons because their parents are unwilling or unable to pay the costs. Because the ability to swim is important in a region with so much water, swimming lessons should be free for every child.

133741.explanation: Large-scale surveys are a better alternative in cases where the municipality wishes to consult with residents.

133742.explanation: The municipality ought to lower taxes and rates for its residents where possible. Our goal is to ensure that taxes, such as the OZB, rise only in line with inflation.

133743.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to gain input from residents – adults and children alike  – via platforms and other organisations prior to policy formation.

133746.explanation: Ground lease can lead to uncertainty and rising costs for home owners. Giving occupants the option to buy out their ground lease voluntarily and under reasonable conditions increases housing security and control over one’s own home. Clear and transparent regulations will be required, however.

133747.explanation: Industry in Zaanstad is important, but there must always remain enough space to build homes.

133748.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that we need to take care of our planet, and that polluting businesses therefore require stricter surveillance.

133749.explanation: Industry is important, but not at the expense of the environment.

133750.explanation: We endeavour to help recognised refugees allocated to Zaanstad find a home within a reasonable time period – preferably within six months.

133751.explanation: We are a socially-minded municipality, this is our strength. Businesses are also very happy with the employees from the asylum-seeker shelters. We should remain in dialogue at local and national level, however, to ensure a fair distribution.

133752.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports the current waste policy, with collection of separated waste and centralised drop-off of landfill waste in neighbourhoods. Larger families, senior citizens, people with disabilities and those living in high-rise buildings must continue to be taken into account.

133753.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants as many sustainable businesses as possible to set up in the area.

133754.explanation: We also believe that there is much more opportunity to generate solar on rooftops and façades than is currently being realised.

133756.explanation: It is important to do everything in our power to achieve these goals as soon as possible, the sooner the better.

133757.explanation: The transition to gas-free living is necessary, but the timeline must be achievable and affordable. The municipality must work towards lower gas consumption and sustainable alternatives, but cannot guarantee that all homes will be gas-free by 2040, as needs vary between neighbourhoods, housing types, and the needs of residents.

133758.explanation: The ChristenUnie is in favour of wind turbines where possible, however there must be effective consultation with surrounding residents beforehand.

133759.explanation: The rationale for this location is not well-founded. While we are in favour of the energy transition, more investigation is necessary to determine whether there really is no alternative.

133760.explanation: Many Zaanstad residents are bothered by the night flights overhead. For the health of the people in our municipality, it is important that these night flights are stopped.

133761.explanation: It is important to the area that sports clubs can set up and remain viable for the future. Municipal financial support is therefore a good thing.

133763.explanation: Culture is important to the ChristenUnie, however we do not want Zaanstad Noord residents’ quality of life to be affected by a permanent event site.

133764.explanation: Amenities such as skate parks, small neighbourhood playing fields, playgrounds, and fitness trails should be a continued focus.

133765.explanation: Staying active is very important, which is why ChristenUnie is in favour of earmarking more funds for sports clubs.

133766.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that everything possible should be done to prevent the demolition and neglect of special buildings.

133767.explanation: Safety in the city is very important. More and more people are feeling unsafe, which is why it is important for BOAs to help increase safety levels.

133768.explanation: Camera surveillance is important, but the ChristenUnie would rather invest these funds in prevention.

133770.explanation: Improved collaboration, education, and a firm action are the keys to reducing weapon possession among young people.

133771.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes this funding should go to prevention and more hangout spots for these young people, giving them somewhere to go other than the streets.

133772.explanation: Many people feel at home living in Krommenie and Wormerveer. It is therefore important for more homes to be built there, for the future generation.

133773.explanation: There is currently a lack of mobility, which is why the ChristenUnie would like to focus on mid-range rental and owner-occupied housing.

133774.explanation: Rewarding residents for greening their gardens can be a good incentive. Greenery helps prevent heat stress and flooding.

133775.explanation: This is very dependent on the building in question: heritage buildings should not be demolished.

133776.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that these services should be accounted for. There is lots of building going on in Zaanstad, but essential services are often forgotten.

133777.explanation: The acute housing shortage means it is important to create living space wherever possible. Subdivision can allow starters on the market to find a home that suits them. Each neighbourhood needs to be evaluated for suitability and feasibility, however.

133778.explanation: It is important for local residents who wish to continue living in Zaanstad to have priority over people from outside the region. This allows Zaanstad residents to maintain their ties with the region and with their friends and family, who often live here too.

133779.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes it is important to build affordable homes for purchase, as it promotes much-needed mobility in the social rental sector.

133780.explanation: Homes are for living in, not investing in. Mandatory owner-occupation keeps the housing market fairer and more accessible to starters and families. It prevents investors from buying up homes, leaving residents without an affordable place to live.

133781.explanation: Industrial estates are meant for industry. Building homes there may expose residents to disturbance from these businesses.

133782.explanation: We would currently prefer to keep building within the city limits for as long as possible. However, we do realise that construction is necessary, so expanding could be considered in future.

133783.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that the municipality should invest more in combating poverty, since nobody should be left behind. We must step up to help the less fortunate in society, and offer them future prospects.

133784.explanation: Having benefit recipients perform community work keeps them involved in society. Solutions must always be tailored, however.

133785.explanation: The ChristenUnie currently does not see the any need for an additional bridge across the Zaan. That money could be better spent on other projects.

133786.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants reliable governance. After multiple false promises to Westerwatering, it is time to put their money where their mouth is.

133787.explanation: Roads must be built with a purpose. Where many additional homes are built in a neighbourhood, we see the value of adding extra roads.

133788.explanation: During the Guisweg project, the ChristenUnie submitted multiple proposals for an underground line. We believe this is the solution to the traffic bottlenecks currently caused by the Zaanlijn train.

133790.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants high-quality public transport, which should include a night train.

133791.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes an alternative to the A8-A9 link should be developed, that addresses the needs of residents and local through-traffic.

133792.explanation: De Vlinder was a bridge made specially for buses, and the Achtersluispolder was never designed with lots of car traffic in mind.

133793.explanation: Parking problems impact neighbourhood liveability, which is why the Zaanstad parking policy should be updated regularly. Neighbourhoods must also be assessed individually, some neighbourhoods need more parking than others.

133794.explanation: The ChristenUnie feels that parking policy has become an end in itself. Our belief is that Zaanstad should be a welcoming municipality, one that is attractive to the shopping public.

133795.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports low-emission zones, but we do not feel the entire municipality should be a low-emission zone.

133796.explanation: The 30km zones must be implemented, they will increase road safety for cyclists and pedestrians.

133797.explanation: Meeting spots for young people are extremely important. It is important for all of Zaanstad’s towns and villages to have places where young people can come together and feel at home.

133798.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes it is important for all of Zaanstad to have places where the elderly can go for social contact, support, and appropriate assistance. This combats loneliness.

133799.explanation: Informal carers should receive more recognition. They are not volunteers, and do their work with love and dedication. The municipality should offer them help and support that is also easy to access.

133800.explanation: All parents should be able to take free parental or antenatal classes.

133801.explanation: With so much water in the Netherlands, it is important for all children to learn to swim. The ChristenUnie therefore believes it should be free.

133741.explanation: Our residents elect parties with a diverse range of views. Our divided council needs lots of parties to form a coalition. The majority of council members do not always represent what most of our residents want. A referendum is a valuable democratic addition.

133742.explanation: Property tax in Zaanstad is high, considering what our residents get in return. The costs should be proportional to what the municipality provides, and not rise any faster than inflation.

133743.explanation: Our municipality is a collection of villages and urban areas, with a range of neighbourhoods. Lokaal Zaans strongly believes that neighbourhood and village councils should give residents a say on municipal plans.

133746.explanation: It is not about a ‘cheap’ option, but about a purchasing scheme that does justice to the conditions under which our residents entered into a ground lease. The scheme should be fair and reasonable, and reached in proper consultation with the Erfpacht Zaanstad interest group.

133747.explanation: The goal is not more industrial estates, it is adequate space for our businesses. Businesses are currently short of space, and so we agree. Our businesses drive our economy, and provide a lot of employment. We must roll out the proverbial red carpet for them.

133748.explanation: Enforcement does not need to be stricter, merely enough to ensure compliance with laws and regulations. We must work with cooperative companies to keep pollution as low as possible (lower than the required minimum!), and deliberate violators must be firmly dealt with.

133749.explanation: Our industry must be given enough space. Our preference is to give potential housing developments, sport and exercise facilities more space in nature areas, where it serves the interests of Zaanstad residents.

133750.explanation: Disagree: our Zaanstad youth deserve priority. They are forced to wait a long time for a home (which is supposed to be a right!) and even postpone having a family because of it. Madness!

133751.explanation: With the current housing shortage and pressure on our facilities (looming doctor shortage, pressure on public spaces, traffic congestion, lack of enforcement, etc.) we must not bite off more than we can chew.

133752.explanation: This paves the way to illegal dumping and littering. The idea sounds good in theory, but in practice it just makes things worse. We are more in favour of post-separation, which has proven to work best in practice.

133753.explanation: What is ‘sustainable’ in this context? And who gets to decide? We should concentrate on attracting businesses that fit with our Zaanstad environment and residents. Of course we are in favour of minimising pollution, and are prepared to collaborate to do so.

133754.explanation: We would rather see cows and grass in fields than solar panels, but we certainly do not wish to prohibit sustainability initiatives that are self-sufficient. In our view, solar panels are best on the roof.

133756.explanation: We're green, but we're not crazy! Targets must be realistic and feasible. Unattainable and/or unaffordable targets have no place in good governance.

133757.explanation: Other European countries are giving grants to people switching to gas. We support sustainability, but phasing out gas should not be an end in itself, especially considering our power-supply problems (network congestion) that will continue for years. National legislation says 2050.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines run on subsidies, and besides providing expensive power, they also disrupt people and the environment.

133759.explanation: Our municipality seems to be the Cinderella of the region. We've been waiting half a century for a motorway – if it gets built at all – but a high-voltage corridor gets shoved down our throats in record time.

133760.explanation: Schiphol is a significant economic driver in our country, and vital to our business climate. The disturbance caused by day and night flights should continue to be addressed, to improve our already-threatened quality of life.

133761.explanation: Sports and other associations are the mortar between the bricks of our society. Recreation promotes health, a sense of community, quality of life, the list goes on. Shrinking volunteer numbers are making it harder and harder for associations to make ends meet. They deserve our support for the important work they do.

133763.explanation: Both large and small events are important, and there are now too few venues where they can be organised. Of course, effective consultation and solid agreements with the surrounding residents and businesses are essential.

133764.explanation: Outdoor sports are becoming extremely popular, and are a wonderful way to exercise and come together in the fresh air. Sport is one of the greatest investments there is!

133765.explanation: Many of our sports fields and buildings are operating beyond capacity. Swimming pools are no exception, and do not meet current standards. A healthy municipality should supply adequate facilities for community and club activities at socially responsible rates – such as sufficient affordable pool water for swimming clubs.

133766.explanation: Heritage is important, as it tells the history of our region. It is our authenticity. Much cultural heritage was demolished in the past: we must prevent that from happening as much as possible in the future! However, we do believe it is important to consult properly with building owners, and to assist with the costs and opportunities for use and sustainability. This may mean that not all historic buildings will necessarily receive monument, landmark, or other protective status; offering development where possible can also help.

133767.explanation: As long as there is a lack of police capacity, BOAs – whose authority has increased in recent years – can take on some of their tasks. We have an enforcement shortfall, which must be addressed.

133768.explanation: High-risk areas require adequate coverage. The council unfortunately voted against one of our proposals. but we will not let it go!

133770.explanation: Even though we may only get a few young people on track this way, youth is our future. These investments must continue.

133771.explanation: Yes, for both repressive measures (responding to disturbances) and preventive measures (e.g. by offering alternatives such as sport and exercise).

133772.explanation: 5000 new homes in Krommenie and Wormerveer is too many. We will need to find other solutions to the housing shortage. Krommenie and Wormerveer cannot cope with this amount – liveability, accessibility, and the historic character of these areas are at stake (priority is not applicable here).

133773.explanation: It's the same everywhere. The Netherlands is already leading the world in terms of social housing, but this will not solve the problem. The issue is a lack of mobility in the housing market, a high influx of starters, and a lack of affordable homes (mid-range purchase and rental).

133774.explanation: Although this should not really be the municipality's concern, we do see the value of free tile collection. Promoting greener gardens improves drainage. The inability of rainwater to drain effectively is increasingly becoming a problem.

133775.explanation: Demolition is certainly not always the best solution. Experts say that existing structures can often easily be renovated and modified. Besides, there has been plenty of demolition in the past, and we do not want our municipality to be full of soulless boxes.

133776.explanation: In our view, these basic facilities are a fundamental pre-requisite for new housing developments, along with quality infrastructure. No more building for building's sake, which is mainly to the detriment of existing residents, but building with vision.

133777.explanation: Although subdivision into rooms seems to make sense, it exacerbates our traffic problems, parking problems, lack of facilities, and so on.

133778.explanation: Our residents are also our heritage, and the youth of today are our future. Of course we should try to help as many people as possible wherever we can, but not to the detriment of our own residents, who are the municipality's responsibility, after all!

133779.explanation: It's the same everywhere. The Netherlands is already leading the world in terms of social housing, but this will not solve the problem. The issue is a lack of mobility in the housing market, a high influx of starters, and a lack of affordable homes. Moreover, developments with a high level of rental social housing are financially not feasible.

133780.explanation: The fact is that this will not help solve the housing shortage. Sometimes the opposite can help – when parents buy a home for their children, for example. There is also a major shortage of rental homes, which will only grow as a result.

133781.explanation: There is already a shortage of business locations. We wish to keep up municipal employment rates, and raise them where possible, through the more efficient use of our industrial estates.

133782.explanation: Agreed, especially for residents in the villages, and in alignment with the history of the village in question.

133783.explanation: Money alone will not solve this problem, however. What matters is enough employment and other opportunities, as well as the desire to work. Unfortunately, working (or working more) does not always pay in the Netherlands, and residents end up disadvantaging themselves if they do.

133784.explanation: Ongoing participation in and contact with our community is of crucial importance to everybody. Just sitting at home is no good to anybody.

133785.explanation: In Wormerland especially, one bridge is no longer enough. The Zaanstreek region has outgrown its infrastructure. The bridge must be part of a broader, integrated mobility plan for the Noord area, however. Without the A8-A9 connection, the N203 will grind to a halt.

133786.explanation: Agree, PROVIDED that suitable adaptations are made to the Westerkoog ring road! Westerkoog residents deserve a safe living environment, which must be a prime consideration.

133787.explanation: Our roads are already filling up, and the municipality just keeps building. We are in favour of realistic developments, with suitable parking facilities and roads for proper traffic flow.

133788.explanation: That any other decision was made is ludicrous. Our train line also largely serves other municipalities. The provincial authorities refuse to accommodate an underground tunnel, but they will push through a high-voltage power corridor happily enough. Our residents will benefit greatly from an underground train line!

133790.explanation: For a relatively low sum, we could join up with other initiatives.

133791.explanation: Our Krommenie and Assendelft residents also deserve clean air and good connectivity. Heavy daily commuter traffic and congestion contribute to an unhealthy environment and lots of disturbance. The national government should keep its promise, and deliver! In the meantime, we must invest in alternatives.

133792.explanation: Opening the Vlinderbrug bridge will improve access to both Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder.

133793.explanation: With the current housing crisis, our children will continue to live at home even longer. Parking is already a problem, removing parking spaces now would be lunacy.

133794.explanation: Paid parking must remain affordable, and only be introduced where strictly necessary. Paid parking must not be a smokescreen to fill the municipal purse.

133795.explanation: There should be NO low-emission zones ANYWHERE in Zaanstad. Not only are they impractical due to network congestion, the impact is minimal and comes at a great expense to our businesses. Our residents will also end up paying more for businesses that go electric – this has already happened elsewhere.

133796.explanation: Agreed where traffic safety will benefit, such as residential zones or locations without separate bicycle lanes, but this is not the case everywhere. Tailored solutions in consultation with residents is our motto.

133797.explanation: A rich and appealing community and club life already attracts a lot of youth, in this we cannot invest enough. ‘Other’ young people also need a place to meet, without harming the surrounding area.

133798.explanation: Every village and neighbourhood needs the necessary facilities, not just a community care centre. Combining facilities, which makes them easier to find and access, also means they remain affordable.

133799.explanation: Informal carers are not a debit item on the balance sheet, but a vital element of our ageing society. We cannot thank them enough for all of the hard work and love they continue to give.

133800.explanation: There is no such thing as ‘free'; here, ‘free’ means that somebody else pays. Support for pregnant people in Zaanstad is generally well organised.

133801.explanation: This statement is misleading. All children in Zaanstad should be safe in the water! And they can be, because swimming lessons are financially accessible through existing schemes. We are in favour of including swimming lessons as part of physical education, with a focus on water safety.

statements.title: A permanent event site should be built in Zaanstad North.
statements.pro: A permanent site makes it easier to organise events. Some parties believe that a suitable, permanent site will limit the impact of events on the surrounding area.
statements.con: Some parties believe it is a waste of space to dedicate a single site entirely to events. Multiple sites would be better, as the inconvenience is distributed across the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should monitor industrial pollution in Zaanstad more strictly.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for monitoring industrial pollution in Zaanstad. It delegates this task to the North Sea Canal Area Environmental Authority (Omgevingsdienst Noordzeekanaalgebied). The Authority monitors whether companies keep to the regulations governing emissions and other environmental aspects.
statements.pro: A clean living environment is important to the health of Zaanstad residents.Stricter monitoring is necessary to protect nature and the environment.
statements.con: Supervision levels should match pollution levels. Monitoring should therefore be in line with the existing legislation, not any stricter.According to some parties, there is no evidence that stricter controls are necessary.

statements.title: The municipal property tax (OZB) should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: A tax paid by the owners of buildings or land, or by the users of business premises.

statements.moreInfo: The municipal property tax (OZB) is an annual tax paid to the municipality by people who own or use a home, building, or piece of land. The amount depends on the value of the property in question (the WOZ value). The municipality ultimately decides how much OZB residents pay.
statements.pro: The OZB is not meant to plug holes in the municipal budget.Some parties believe the OZB in Zaanstad is too high.
statements.con: If residents pay less OZB, the municipality will have less to spend on public services, such as cultural activities, greenery, and healthcare.The OZB in Zaanstad is not high, some parties say.

statements.title: The municipality should be able to ask residents for their opinion on major decisions via a referendum.

titleParts.text: The municipality should be able to ask residents for their opinion on major decisions via a

titleParts.glossary: A public vote in which residents can give their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not legally bound to implement the outcome.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum, the government asks voters a question on a single topic. Residents can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum, all Zaanstad residents aged 16 or over are allowed to vote. A referendum can be requested either by the municipal council or by ordinary residents. If citizens want one, they must gather signatures from other residents. The municipal regulations state how many signatures are needed. In the Netherlands, the results of a referendum are not ‘binding,’ which means that the government can ignore the result and decide to do something else.
statements.pro: This will involve residents in important decisions that affect the place where they live.Referendums are democratic.Some parties believe that referendums are a good way to help generate solutions to problems.
statements.con: Referendums oversimplify complex decisions.Residents can already vote on a party every four years, and therefore already have enough representation in the municipal council.Some parties believe that organising a referendum for every major decision is too expensive and complicated.

statements.title: Residential construction in Zaanstad should focus on mid-range rental and owner-occupied housing.
statements.moreInfo: Mid-range homes are rental or owner-occupied homes that are priced just above the limit for social housing, intended for middle-income earners who earn too much for social housing, but who cannot afford an expensive home.
statements.pro: The problem in the housing market is the lack of mobility. Mid-range rentals are needed to allow people in rental social housing to move on, freeing up homes for others.More mid-range owner-occupied homes will encourage seniors to move out of their cheaper homes, freeing them up for others.
statements.con: What Zaanstad needs most is more affordable housing, as the housing shortage is greatest among those seeking rental social housing.A good blend of cheaper and mid-range homes plus social rentals is essential.

statements.title: Zaanstad residents should pay each time they use underground waste containers.
statements.moreInfo: Currently, recyclables (such as paper and green waste) are collected separately in Zaanstad. Residents can dispose of landfill (general waste) in underground waste containers. Currently, annual waste-collection fees are the same for every household, regardless of the size or how often they use the underground containers.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that Zaanstad produces too much landfill waste. If there is a reward for less waste, residents will recycle more and there will be less landfill.It is only fair for residents and businesses who produce more waste to pay more.
statements.con: This will only make residents dump their waste illegally to avoid having to pay, creating a mess on the street.Some parties find it unfair for large families and people with a lot of medical waste to have to pay more.

statements.title: Zaanstad should not accept any more asylum seekers than is legally required. Currently, the municipality does accept more than its minimum quota.

titleParts.text: Zaanstad should not accept any more

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision on their case.

titleParts.text: than is legally required. Currently, the municipality does accept more than its minimum quota.

statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad hosts 1550 asylum seekers, while the Distribution Act only requires 818. This is the number that the national government has allocated to Zaanstad. 1550 is more than that, but there is a reason for it. Municipalities in the Zaanstreek-Waterland region have agreed to share out the numbers among themselves. It was agreed that Zaanstad will provide asylum-seeker accommodation. In return, the municipality of Purmerend will make sure it takes in the required number of Ukrainian refugees, while the smaller municipalities will house unaccompanied minor asylum seekers.
statements.pro: Accepting more than the legal requirement will be to the detriment of existing residents, as facilities will be under strain.More asylum seekers means more community disruption, some parties say.It is time for other municipalities to step up and do their part.
statements.con: It is socially-minded and compassionate for Zaanstad to take on more asylum seekers than the minimum.Accommodating asylum seekers does not cause any disruption, some parties say, so there is no reason to take on fewer.Some parties say that businesses are extremely happy with the employees from the asylum-seeker shelters.

statements.title: The municipality should combat the subdivision of houses into rented rooms.

titleParts.glossary: Splitting up a house into smaller rooms and renting each room separately.

statements.moreInfo: Property owners must apply to the municipality of Zaanstad if they wish to rent or sell a home separately as individual rooms. The municipality can decide whether to issue the permit, and under what conditions (only for houses of a certain size, for example).
statements.pro: Subdivision creates disruption for the surrounding residents.
statements.con: Subdivision creates additional housing, for young people and other groups.

statements.title: The municipality should allow solar panels on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality only has partial influence on whether solar panels can be installed on agricultural land. Provincial and national legislation also applies.
statements.pro: Sustainable energy production contributes to our climate targets.Solar panels will help reduce the power shortage.
statements.con: Solar panels on agricultural land damage the landscape.Agricultural land is intended for farmers to produce food.

statements.title: The municipality should fund a night train that runs between Zaanstad and Amsterdam.
statements.pro: A night train increases safety when travelling between Amsterdam and Zaanstad at night.A night train is relatively inexpensive, some parties say. There is already a night train between Amsterdam and Alkmaar, that only needs to stop in Zaanstad.
statements.con: There is no need for a night train, as there is already a night bus with many stops. Some parties believe it will be costly to have a night train stop in Zaanstad.

statements.title: The municipality should budget more for improved clubhouses and playing fields for sports associations.
statements.moreInfo: The ‘Participation in Sport’ (Meedoen in sport) scheme allows sports clubs to apply for funding from the municipality: to maintain or renovate their fields, for example.
statements.pro: Sport and exercise promote good health.
statements.con: Sports clubs need to be self-sufficient.

statements.title: Zaanstad should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focusing on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who work for the municipality and monitor whether citizens and companies are following the rules.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for enforcement of minor infringements. These include parking tickets, incorrect bicycle parking, or littering. People who are caught are given a fine. The people who do this work are called ‘municipal enforcement officers,’ or BOAs in Dutch.  Police officers are for general enforcement, and have different duties and authorisations. There are also BOAs who do not work for the municipality, such as those on trains.
statements.pro: BOAs help create a safer city.Some parties say there are too few police officers on the streets. BOAs can help ensure that enforcement in Zaanstad remains adequate.
statements.con: Some parties say that BOAs are not the way to increase safety, and that other measures are necessary.Instead of more BOAs, more effective deployment of existing BOAs would be better.

statements.title: Zaanstad should give homeowners a cheap option to buy out their ground lease.

titleParts.text: Zaanstad should give homeowners a cheap option to buy out their

titleParts.glossary: A system that allows the municipality to retain ownership of land. Homeowners pay a lease to the municipality for use of the land, this is called the ‘ground rent.’

statements.moreInfo: Owner-occupied homes are not always sold with the land underneath them. Sometimes the land is owned by someone else, usually the municipality. People who buy such homes do so under ‘ground lease,’ and must pay an annual fee for use of the land. This annual payment is called the ‘ground rent.’ Homeowners can sometimes buy out their ground rent for a number of years. If they sell their home, the new owner does not need to pay the ground rent until this period expires.
statements.pro: For homeowners, ground lease means financial stress and a lack of security.
statements.con: Ground lease gives the municipality control over what happens to the land.

statements.title: Industry in Zaanstad should expand into areas that are currently green spaces.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad is currently looking to expand industrial zones through development within the existing zones. Examples are HoogTij and the Achtersluispolder.
statements.pro: Industry creates jobs, including for Zaanstad residents.
statements.con: Nature contributes to city liveability, and deserves protection.

statements.title: The municipality should designate as many historical buildings as possible as monuments.
statements.moreInfo: A ‘monument’ is a building, area, or object of historic, architectural, or scientific value that is protected by the government. There are national, provincial, and municipal monuments.  To make almost any kind of change to a monument, the owner requires a permit first.
statements.pro: Heritage is important, because historic buildings embody the history of our region.  Many significant buildings have been demolished in the past: this can be prevented through allocation of monument status.The protection of buildings as historic monuments makes Zaanstad a nicer place to live for residents.
statements.con: Historic buildings can also be preserved without monument status.Official monuments are very costly to the municipality and property owners.Monument status limits the modifications possible to buildings, when they sometimes need to be modified, or demolished to make way for new buildings. To meet modern standards.

statements.title: Zaanstad should install more security cameras in the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad currently has cameras in the nightlife area of Zaandam (including the Dam and the surrounding streets), partly around Zaandam station, and a number of cameras at Krommenie-Assendelft station. The cameras were placed by the municipality to ensure public order and safety. This means that the municipality can use the cameras to monitor activity in those areas. They are not surveillance cameras used by the Public Prosecution Service to detect and investigate criminal offences.
statements.pro: Security cameras increase safety, according to some parties.Security cameras allow criminals to be apprehended, some parties say.
statements.con: Some parties believe that security cameras only provide the illusion of security.Security cameras are an invasion of privacy on Zaanstad residents.

statements.title: The municipality should fund free antenatal classes for Zaanstad residents who are expecting a child.
statements.moreInfo: An antenatal class usually consists of several evening sessions, where expectant parents receive information on pregnancy, childbirth, and parenthood.  Some such courses are covered by supplementary health insurance. The Zaanstad municipal health service (GGD) already offers free antenatal classes for people on minimum wage.
statements.pro: This will allow more expectant parents to prepare properly for the arrival of their child.This helps ensure equal opportunities for all children in Zaanstad.A good start in life is essential for children's healthy development.
statements.con: Free antenatal classes are often already available from the Municipal Public Health Service (GGD).It is reasonable to expect a level of independence from residents, and information on pregnancy and parenthood is already available to expectant parents in Zaanstad.Some parties believe this is unnecessary meddling.

statements.title: The municipality should allow buildings to be demolished to make way for new buildings.
statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad currently allows buildings to be demolished to make way for new buildings. Exceptions include buildings with monument status, landmark buildings, and village conservation sites.
statements.pro: Demolition is sometimes required for new developments that meet modern standards.Demolishing old buildings and replacing them with new ones allows more homes to be created.
statements.con: Renovation is preferable, in order to preserve landmark buildings.Renovation is cheaper than demolition.

statements.title: At least 40% of new builds in Zaanstad should be social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: Op dit moment is 30% van de huizen in de gemeente Zaanstad een sociale huurwoning. Dat is ongeveer hetzelfde als het landelijke percentage.
statements.pro: What Zaanstad needs is more rental social housing, as the housing shortage is greatest among those seeking this kind of accommodation.According to some parties, building more rental social housing will contribute to neighbourhood diversity.
statements.con: Zaanstad has a relatively high number of residents on low incomes, which puts pressure on services. The problem in the housing market is the lack of mobility. Eliminating the long waiting lists for rental social housing requires more mid-range rentals.

statements.title: The municipality should educate young people on the dangers of owning and using stabbing weapons.
statements.pro: It is important to prevent young people, who are under lots of social pressure, to use knives to commit crime or violence. Education can help, some parties say.
statements.con: Possession of some stabbing weapons is prohibited, and their use for crime or violence is punishable by law. The focus should therefore be on seizing the weapons and punishing perpetrators.

statements.title: People should perform community service or complete study in return for their benefit payments.

titleParts.glossary: This can include cleaning public spaces, or providing companionship and support to senior citizens.

titleParts.text: or complete study in return for their benefit payments.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and receive no other welfare payments can apply for unemployment benefit from the municipality. Some municipalities require that recipients do something in return. In these cases, they perform community service without payment.
statements.pro: This helps people to continue to participate in society and find paid work.Some parties believe it is reasonable to ask something in return for welfare benefits.
statements.con: Not everybody on benefit is able to perform community service or undertake study.Mandatory mutual participation increases stress and distrust.

statements.title: The Zaans Climate Agreement states that the municipality should be climate-neutral by 2040. Zaanstad should try to reach that goal sooner.

titleParts.text: The Zaans Climate Agreement states that the municipality should be

titleParts.glossary: The point where the municipality as a whole no longer contributes to climate change through CO2 emissions, etc.

titleParts.text: by 2040. Zaanstad should try to reach that goal sooner.

statements.moreInfo: An energy-neutral or climate-neutral municipality generates just as much clean energy as residents and businesses consume. The Zaans Climate Agreement has set a target to become climate-neutral between 2030 and 2040. To do so, Zaanstad must reduce its CO2 and other greenhouse-gas emissions that are released through the use of gas, electricity, and fossil fuels. Residents also cause indirect emissions through manufacturing, travel, and homes. Most emissions, however, come from industry. If things continue as they are, Zaanstad estimates its emissions will be halved by 2040. Reaching climate neutrality is a challenge.
statements.pro: The climate crisis means that Zaanstad needs to be climate-neutral as soon as possible.This will enhance residents’ living environment.Some parties believe that a climate-neutral municipality will help residents financially.
statements.con: Zaanstad's contribution to the global climate crisis is negligible.Some parties believe it is not feasible to reach climate neutrality sooner than 2040.Some parties believe it will cost residents more.

statements.title: The Vlinderbrug bridge between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder in Zaandam should be open to cars.
statements.moreInfo: The bridge between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder is meant for public-transport buses. Cyclists and pedestrians can also use it.
statements.pro: Car traffic in Zaandam Zuid will become smoother and faster if cars can use the Vlinderbrug bridge.This will increase safety.
statements.con: This will create more cut-through traffic.The bridge is meant for public transport, bicycles, and pedestrians, which will suffer if they have to share the bridge with cars.

statements.title: Whoever buys a house in our municipality should live in it.
statements.moreInfo: The national government allows municipalities to introduce ‘purchase protection’ for homes up to a certain value.  Anyone who buys such a home must live in it themselves. Leasing the home is only possible in certain situations, such as parents who buy a home for their child. Zaanstad already has purchase protection for homes up to €420,000, which is the national affordability threshold.
statements.pro: According to some parties: homes are for living in, not for making a profit.It creates fairness in the housing market. Home buyers will not need to bid against investors, who drive up the prices.
statements.con: What people do with their houses is their own business, the government has no say in the matter.Buying for rental can help combat the housing shortage, as there is also a shortage of rental housing.

statements.title: Paid parking in Zaanstad should be cheaper.
statements.pro: The municipality should not use paid parking just to make money. Zaanstad should be welcoming to visitors.Parking is a basic necessity.
statements.con: This will increase parking problems. Parking fees in Zaanstad are not high compared to other cities. People should be discouraged from using their cars.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more to combat poverty.
statements.pro: Living in poverty creates health problems and stress.Living in poverty results in debt and social exclusion. The municipality has a responsibility to help the disadvantaged in society.
statements.con: According to some parties, there is no evidence that more funding is necessary to combat poverty.Money should not go directly to poorer residents, it should be spent more effectively. A better way for the municipality to combat poverty would be to invest in education, for example.

statements.title: The ‘caregiver allowance’ for informal carers should be raised from 50 to 140 Euros per year.

titleParts.glossary: People who provide unpaid and often long-term care to family members or friends who are unwell.

titleParts.text: should be raised from 50 to 140 Euros per year.

statements.moreInfo: Each year, Zaanstad gives informal carers an annual token of appreciation for their efforts. This is called the ‘caregiver allowance.’ Carers aged 18 and over receive €50; those aged between 6 and 17 receive a voucher for €30. To qualify, people must be Zaanstad residents and provide at least 6 hours of care per week within a three-month period, or 250 hours per year, with no financial compensation.
statements.pro: Informal carers are essential in our ageing society, which is why they should be rewarded more.Informal carers save the municipality money, by easing pressure on professional care services.
statements.con: Informal carers do not need money, they need a break. The municipality would be better off investing more in respite care, such as day care programmes, to ease the burden on informal caregivers.An increase from €50 to €140 per year does nothing to show more appreciation for informal carers.

statements.title: The municipality should allow construction on the outskirts of existing villages.
statements.moreInfo: In partnership with the province, the municipality decides on building permits, city expansion, and/or rezoning in the Zaanstad landscape. There is increasing demand for expansion and new housing, including on the outskirts of villages. Zaanstad is committed to building many more homes. But what locations are suitable, and which are absolutely off-limits? How can the unique character of the landscape be preserved? The municipality has some influence on these decisions, but they are also governed by provincial policy, which only permits minor expansion in the valuable provincial countryside.
statements.pro: Zaanstad needs more housing, and space is limited.
statements.con: The green areas around Zaanstad must be protected.

statements.title: Recognized refugees should be given priority for local social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country due to war or persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application, and decides whether they can stay in the Netherlands. If the IND approves, refugees receive residency status. That is why recognized refugees are called ‘status holders’ in Dutch. Every six months, the national government orders municipalities to accommodate a certain number of these refugees. Municipalities are free to decide whether to prioritise them for housing or not. Currently around 8% of rental social housing in Zaanstad goes to recognized refugees.
statements.pro: All vulnerable groups in need of housing should qualify for priority.Some parties say that the number of rental social homes going to recognised refugees in Zaanstad is not very high, and so granting priority is no problem.
statements.con: Recognised refugees should be treated the same as everybody else, which means no priority.Recognised refugees do not deserve priority – young people in Zaanstad do.

statements.title: The Zaan river needs an extra bridge.
statements.moreInfo: There are currently multiple bridges across the Zaan, including the Zaanbrug. The construction of thousands of new homes in places such as Wormer, Wormerveer, and Krommenie is causing a rise in traffic. This is why an additional bridge across the Zaan is being considered, for both Zaanstad and Wormerland. There are no concrete plans as yet, but the bridge would go between Wormer and Wormerveer.
statements.pro: Traffic is becoming congested, an additional bridge is needed.Better traffic flow is safer.
statements.con: We do not need another bridge across the Zaan.We shouldn't be making things any easier for cars in Zaanstad.

Zaanstad should oppose the placement of high-voltage pylons in the Assendelft and Westzaan polders.
Zaanstad should urge the national and provincial governments to build the A8/A9 connection after all.
Zaanstad should put more pressure on the national government to eliminate night flights to and from Schiphol.
Fewer than the planned 5000 new homes should be built in Krommerie and Wormerveer.
Zaanstad needs more industrial estates.
The municipal should support sports associations in financial difficulty.

The municipality should reward citizens who turn their gardens into green spaces and remove tiles.
Industrial estates should also include homes.
The municipality should not build any more roads for cars.
The Zaanlijn railroad should be partially underground.

Every town in Zaanstad should have a community care centre for seniors.
